Title: Is the Lack of Empathy in Children a Taboo or a Reality?
Paragraph: Defining the term Evil has been one of the most challenging tasks throughout the human history. Being considered as the counterpart of good, it seems almost impossible to extinguish it thoroughly. On a philosophical ground good and evil would be dichotomies, however, in real life people tend to follow the path of good and reject the evil. It is not hard to imagine why people decide that being good should be the only possible way, as being evil would destroy the society. Nonetheless, various reasons lead some people to become evil. At this point, one must not forget that an evil person was once a child too. Therefore, it is essentially important to understand the development of "evil people" through the early stages of their lives if we want to understand the reason why they decide to go against the society. Therefore, I will try to elaborate on the modern definitions of evil, focusing on evil at child development stage, and putting the objective on society's role on this matter .

Title: Some people say that in our modern world, dominated by science and technology and industrialisation, there is no longer a place for dreaming and imagination. What is your opinion?
Paragraph: If we are to assume that technological progress and human imagination are two irreconcilable things, then we would imminently affiliate ourselves to the Romantic conceptions or even the Luddite attitude. This rage-against-the-machine phenomenon is on a par with primitive myth - making. Moreover, the trends which trigger this kind of attitude can even be traced back to a cheap horror story. Why? The answer here is fear - fear of the unknown. Just like Medusa in the myths of the ancient Greeks, the embodiment of ultimate evil, or even the gruesome monster in Bram Stoner's novel. These two examples both lead us to one and the same conclusion. People tend to generalize their fear in a flat black-and-white image. This also holds true for the stereotypes that have been built up to sum up the three-dimensional technological reality into a distorted one-dimensional concept. And what happens if this image is reduced to a terrifyingly intelligent machine which lurks the infinite paths of the informational highway? From this starting point it is not hard to jump to the conclusion that technologies may impair our mental abilities .

Title: Public speaking is done in the public tongue, the national or tribal language; and the language of our tribe is the men's language. Of course, women can learn it. We're not dumb. Paragraph: To understand why the reality is so unfair to women, let us consider conditions in which men and women have endued and what social roles they had in the past. From the early times men engaged in hunting, whereas the role of women was confined to bringing up and <?> of children as well as maintaining social ties. Being responsible for provision of food for the family to survive and constantly facing danger and harsh conditions, the man had to be specific and precise in his speech. He had no time and possibility to deepen into various pragmatic manners such as (see page 2) politeness, establishing <?> with interlocutors. He simply gave or obeyed the orders women, on the other hand, focused on building and maintaining social relationships with other members of the community. They made this under pieceful conditions, so they concentrated more on emotional bonds with the hearer, showing empathy and avoiding disagreement. As a result, when a woman has to speak publicly, she is too mild and tentative towards her audience which as if shows that she is not confident in what she is saying and thus does not produce a desirable effect used to giving commands and exercise power through language succeed better in speaking publicly .

Title: Chemistry of Feelings

Scientists treat the state of being in love as a chemical process taking place in the limbical lobe in the brain, thus devoiding it of the romantic mist . According to brain chemists, the "sensation of stupefaction" is nothing more than the effect of production of hormones, which, released by the human brain, evoke the feeling of enamourment. There originates a chain of successive chemical reactions in the organism, which cause euphory and make a man commit great nonsenses in the name of the overwhelming love... Still, a mighty army of chemists, biochemists, biologists, neurologists, psychiatrists and psychologists, despite of being equipped with modern appliances, have not quite explained why one is subject to the state of "amorous infatuation." Title: Mass media affect our approach to reality

This had lasted for ages, until the media were invented, which happened not long ago, at the very last minute, in fact. Homo sapiens appeared on the Earth in 200,000 BC, in 10,000 BC people started to cultivate the land, in 776 BC the first competitors entered the ancient Olympics, and thanks to Johann Gutenberg the first printing machine appeared in circa 1445 AD. Even if the printing technique were considered to be one of the media its influence on the history of the mankind has been relatively short. Title: Should video games be violent or not?
Paragraph: Regarding the first aspect, some studies explored the connection between violent video games and human aggression. The first study <R> consisted of the participants being divided into two groups in which one group would play violent video games and the other non-violent video games for three days for twenty minutes. After that the winners of the competitions were able to blow off their opponents with a loud sound. The results of this study showed that those players who played violent video games blew off the competition with the noise much longer than the ones who played the non-violent video games. Another team from Brock University in Canada also did a study in which they examined 1492 children aging eight to eighteen and discovered that those who played violent video games for a longer period had developed and increased their aggressive behavior. Title: Women in the Koran.
Paragraph: The first thing the Koran did was to abolish the custom of burying baby girls alive: <*> . There were various reasons why baby girls were buried alive. First and foremost, men were required to defend their tribes. In pre-Islamic times, inter-tribal blood feuds were very common, and consequently the Islamic people preferred boys over girls, because only boys could assist in defending the tribe once they grew up. Moreover, it was common for the enemy to take women as prisoners, for whom they could ask large ransoms, which usually consisted of camels and goats. If a tribe did not pay this ransom, their captured women and hence the men who were left behind were dishonoured. In either case the tribe was at a disadvantage and so it was better not to have any girls. A third important reason was that if a daughter stayed alive and was not abducted by another tribe she had to be given away in marriage. While marriage undoubtedly played a very important role in procreation and was therefore of vital importance to the tribe's future, the idea of giving away a daughter in marriage was somehow unattractive to many people. As a result, the birth of a baby girl was often regarded as a disgrace and daughters were looked upon as a liability. An easy way out was to kill a daughter as soon as she was born. The Koran refers to this custom by saying: <*> .

Title: In his novel Animal Farm, George Orwell wrote "All men are equal: but some are more equal than others" How true is this today?
Paragraph: George Orwell, in his immensely popular allegorical novella Animal Farm (1945), satirizes the political situation in pre-war Europe; especially the communist regime of the Soviet Union, whose dictator was Joseph Stalin. Alluding to the biblical story of The Ten Commandments, whose inscriptions were the law of God; the animals, once they take charge of the farm, they write their own commandments (the Seven Commandments), which are laws they should respect. The most striking one at the beginning is "All animals are equal". However, it gradually becomes clear that certain animals do not abide by the laws initially inscribed. By the end of the novella, this quote is modified, "All animals are equal, but some are more equal than others". This is generally seen as addressing the prevalence of inequality within the Soviet's totalitarian regime, while strongly criticizing its leaders and the high classes who were "more equal than others". In contrast to the animal's initial idea of a utopian society, 70 years later and to this very day, societies all over the world are faced with various kinds of inequalities. Unfortunately, equality has had a harder time being utilized than most other democratic facets. Presently, there are nations that have utilized the totalitarian regime, where people are not equal, nations that put on a democratic facade, where some people are more equal than others, and nations where people are equal before the law.

Title: Tolerance
Paragraph: "Tolerance" originates from the Latin verb tolerare which originally meant to bear, endure and suffer. Gradually the meaning of this word has widened and ameliorated, receiving the quality of allowing other people to have their own attitudes and beliefs or to behave in a particular way. During the period of Reformation tolerance became a wide-spread concept concerning especially religious matters. Martin Luther, the leader of the German Protestants, fought for religious freedom, for the acceptance of Protestantism on principle beside the Catholic Church. In the era of the Enlightenment tolerance was also considered as freedom of conscience and personal freedom, not only as religious freedom. The term "tolerance" was one of the key words of this period. Tolerance and its social implications were regarded as the base for a free and open development of individual and personal criticism. To be treated tolerantly became, at least theoretically, part of a treatment in accordance with human rights. Nevertheless, even nowadays, almost five centuries later, tolerance seems to be more difficult to achieve than ever .

Title: All armies should consist entirely of professional soldiers: there is no value in a system of military service
Paragraph: There is another side to the understanding of the value of the system of military service. For centuries, authorities in charge have applied the mandatory enlisting of people to their armies in order to protect themselves and their countries. This is still the case nowadays especially in the Middle East where countries like Syria and Israel rely upon this system to establish a stout defense from the hostile neighbors. Moreover, many people consider enlisting in the army to be an act of loyalty and patriotism. It is viewed as noble, brave and patriotic to serve as a soldier. Many believe that armies should not consist entirely of professional soldiers, on the contrary, people who are fit should sign up and help in keeping their country safe. Along with instilling national loyalty within the youth, the system of military service can arguably make the young people more disciplined, mentally stronger, more mature and some go as far as saying that it is a way to keep the young males from unemployment and the streets. In his piece called The Moral Equivalent of War, William James notes that <*> <R> . Basically, moral motives such as loyalty, patriotism and the idea that serving for one's nation is considered noble and it brings discipline, mental strength and maturity in the youth. These are considered to be the notions that approve the existence of a military service system.

Title: The portrayal of AIDS in the media (1983-1985)
Paragraph: Media Representation, Perceptions of Risk and Helath Education At the beginning of this essay, it was suggested that how people assess their own personal level of risk within the context of a disease such as AIDS depends on who and how many people are perceived to be affected, what the causes of the disease are understood to be, and how the disease is believed to be transmitted. Risk assessment in relation to disease prevention as in any other context involves the dual process of estimating the probabilities of various outcomes and evaluating their consequences. In relation to AIDS, this requires thinking intelligently about low probability events with serious consequences. This is no easy task for those well practised in health-related decision-making, let alone for ordinary men and women, and an essential thing for this kind of thought is good information .

Title: Everybody should have a free travelling card
Paragraph: The first problem which will arise when everyone gets a free travelling card will be the costs. The free travelling card will stimulate the travel by public transport but the current public transport network is unable to cope with an major increase of travellers. In order to transport all the people more buses, trains and trams would have to be brought into action. This increase in the number of public transport vehicles would require larger bus and railway stations. These stations would have to be build or the existing ones would have to be rebuild. But increasing the capacity of the public transport network is not all that would have to be done in order to get people to use this network. To stimulate the use of the network it would have to be made more convenient and attractive. This could be done by making the public transport vehicles ride more frequently and ride to more locations. In this way the public would have more freedom in deciding when to travelling and where to travel to and the using the free travel card would become more attractive. The extension of the public transport network, which would have to go with the free travelling card, would cost a lot and these expense would have to be paid from the proceeds of the travelling card. The first argument against the travelling card would therefore be that it would cost the public too much. In short, in order to make the free travelling card effective the public transport would have to be expanded and this would raise the cost of the card to a level unacceptable to the public .

Title: English is increasingly becoming a world language. Why do you think this is so? What should  be the characteristics of a world language?

The reasons for this enormous increase in the number of speakers after that are quite obvious. The most important phase in the progress of English becoming a world language was the new English speaking settlements in Northern America at at the end of 16th century. From that onwards has American English (the variety of English spoken in North America) had a great impact on spreading the language all over the world and today 215 million of the 300 million native speakers of English are American. The rest consists of British, Irish, Australians, New Zelanders, South Africans and Canadians. In other words, the United States has a major influence on the English language today .

Title: The knowledge gap in society.

Television has the potential of reaching people who could not be reached before; television could therefore provide them with the necessary knowledge of the positions of the candidates on various issues for voting intelligently. On the one hand, making higher education free would give everyone an equal opportunity for further study and development. Title: Dialects as languages

These variations, called dialects, are either the result of a different development of the same original language or a social variation. Thus a dialect can be ' accepted ' as a language, that is, put on the same level of the standard language, but it would cause competition, since every region would like its own language to be the standard one .

Title: What should be done about the ozone hole ?

Title: Europe 93: loss of sovereignty or birth of a nation?

There is a lot of talk about giving more authority to the "regions" of Europe but there is little talk about permitting more sovereignity to immigrant groups who have no spatial identity within Europe. Nor does anybody suggest, at least in the integration discussion, that women, men, gays, lesbians or children should have more say in the decisions concerning them respectively. How much, then, is really changing? Not much. Territory remains the basis of political sovereignity. To go back to my first question it is indeed surprising that we are terrified. There is a predictability that we can still rely on: geography. The peoples of Europe go on taking it for granted that people occupying the same territory have so much in common that they can count on everybody's interests being taken into account as long as they elect the power holders among them. The fact that I certainly have more in common with a Zambian political science student than with a Finnish opera singer nor the fact that I worry more about my Japanese friend than I worry about all these Finns that I have never met is hardly going to interfere and complicate things in the near future .

Title: Is Face-to-Face Learning Better than Online Learning?
Paragraph: The development of the digital era has exponentially taken part in our daily routines, hence almost everything that we have access to in real physical world is transferred into the visual environment interwoven by Internet webs. We started to regulate our bank accounts online and purchasing items online through debit and credit cards has significantly saved time in the lives of many, and additionally the stress caused by waiting in lines is certainly reduced. Shockingly may it sound but nowadays you can find a partner online via various social networks free of charge, and even some sites go to that extent where they create robotic characters based on the personality traits of humans. Admittedly, this seemingly entertaining loving experience is preferred by some individuals and they would rather become attached to a robotic character since it is money saving and less time-consuming. Therefore, if all abovementioned is taken into account the apparent follow-up, since almost everything has online substitution, would be that even the education can substantially take part in the virtual world. There are people who would never forsake the old traditional face-to-face meetings in classroom, however high technology has become that sort of magic that is present in the fairytales, and we must certainly acclaim the benefits from high technology, including the online classes. Nowadays, many classes take place online and there is a various scope of universities that offer BA, MA and even PhD programs. However, the question that follows is whether online learning can substitute the traditional face-to-face learning and is face-to-face model still a better option than the online bandwidth. Several aspects that constitute both of these types of learning need to be taken into account and elaborated thoroughly, however, the kind of answer that will prevail towards face-to-face or online learning cannot be obtained since it highly depends on the choice made by the learner, whether the learner's abilities are suitable for the innovative online or the traditional face-to-face learning. Nevertheless, by constructing an objective list a partial conclusion may be obtained as to whether face-to-face is better than online learning .

Title: Is freedom of speech an illusion?
Paragraph: One of the most pressing social issues of our time is determining where to draw the line between the right to freedom of speech and the right to not be discriminated against. Or, for that matter, where does personal opinion end and hate speech begins? Those concepts don't have any clearly defined boundaries to speak of. If freedom of speech is a basic human right, then it stands to reason that it should not be limited in the name of minimizing inflammatory speech. The thing is, not being discriminated against is also a basic human right. As such, it should not be quashed for the purposes of maintaining freedom of speech. The question of the decade is what happens when those two rights clash, as they so often do in this day and age. Most of the western world currently deals with the issue by imposing legislations against hate speech and discrimination. That model might work in the short term, but the problem is that it can only go on for so long before it gets out of hand. For one, it is absolutely impossible to filter out all offensive words by following that model. Given the right audience, any speech can be hate speech. Everyone is offended by something. For two, censoring free speech is a slippery slope. If it's okay to limit what people are allowed to say in the name of not offending anyone, then how long before it becomes okay to limit other individual choices? On the other hand, discrimination is not an issue that can be swept aside. Something has to be done about it, and if that means limiting freedom of speech in the short term, then that's going to have to work until there is a different solution .

Title: Those who envision a future world speaking only one tongue, whether English, German, Russian or any other, hold a misguided ideal and would do the evolution of the human mind the greatest disservice. Western culture has made, through language, a provisional analysis of reality and, without correctives, hold resolutely to that analysis as final. The only correctives lie in all those other tongues, which by aeons of independent evolution have arrived at different, but equally logical, provisional analyses. (B. L. Whorf)
Paragraph: Linguist Benjamin Lee Whorf opposed the idea of an absolute and final reality. He claimed that language and reality constantly influence each other, thus shaping our perception of the world. Therefore, speakers of Western languages will have a different outlook on the world than speakers of Native American or Native African languages. Indeed, the language that one speaks and one's perception of the world are highly specific. They can be conditioned by the system of religious or magic beliefs, the surrounding environment or cultural reality .

Title: Those who envision a future world speaking only one tongue, whether English, German, Russian or any other, hold a misguided ideal and would do the evolution of the human mind the greatest disservice. Western culture has made, through language, a provisional analysis of reality and, without correctives, hold resolutely to that analysis as final. The only correctives lie in all those other tongues, which by aeons of independent evolution have arrived at different, but equally logical, provisional analyses. (B. L. Whorf)
Paragraph: One language for the whole world means no changes in any location. This is the consequence of different sociological (cultural, political, geographic, etc) conditions which effect in more or less closed environment, such as society. E.g. vocabulary is adjusted to the living environment of the society. Take pigeon languages - they evolve into creole because continuously changing environment must be reflected in all the systems of language. Phonological changes take place, elaborated terminology is created for elaborated spheres of life, cultural traditions are preserved and continued through language .

Title: The prison system is outdated. No civilized society should punish its criminals: it should rehabilitate them

According to this approach, criminals do not commit offenses because they are bad by nature, but rather it is because of the society which forced them to be who they are. How dangerous can accepting this point of view be, may be illustrated by a popular case of a murderer accused in nineteenth century Germany of killing somebody. He said to the court that he was sorry for having committed the crime, but he couldn't be punished, because he had been brought up by the people who regarded crime as something natural and accepted, and therefore the society in general is to be blamed for what had happened. Now we must ask ourselves this question: should we allow killers to get away with a not guilty verdict? Of course not! The judge in the case supported this point of view. He said to the accused that he was sorry, but he, in turn, had been brought up by the people who despised crime and he returned the death sentence verdict .

Title: Public speaking is done in the public tongue, the national or tribal language; and the language of our tribe is the men's language. Of course, women can learn it. We're not dumb. Paragraph: Language has been a powerful tools since prehistoric times when men developed a more or less systematic linguistic structure of using sound they utter and signs they draw. A man could not only express his needs, feelings, knowledge through language but also affect other people primarily by a spoken mode of language. Usually great orators would become leaders of a tribe or of some other form of social community as they knew how to appeal to their comrades' hearts and minds by means of using the right words. Women, on the other hand, would be among listeners as their social role was that of an obedient servant either of a father or (later on, after a marriage) of a husband. It could be thus noted on the rightness in saying that males rule the world wherein they have the primary right to talk. Such an order was explicitly observed in earlier times; nonetheless, it is applicable to the present world. Today a woman needs to master a man's language in order to climb up the social ladder for a man's language is thought to be a perfect tool of persuasion, a sign of self-confidence and thus a path to success .

Title: English is increasingly becoming a world language. Why do you think this is so? What should  be the characteristics of a world language?
Paragraph: What then should be the characteristics of a world language? It should be universally known and universally intelligible. The different varieties of it should not be too diverse to be unerstood by all the users of the language. If the varieties are very differentiated, there should be one form intelligible to all. English meets the demands: It is widely spread and known. There are more than 1,000 million English speakers in the world today . <R> The figure includes native speakers as well as those with lower level of skills. It is either "dominant or well established in all six continents", Crystal further states. However, there are differentiated forms of English that are not perfectly intelligible in all parts of the world. The main differences occur in the vocabulary because of the variance in the reality around the speakers of the the different varieties. For example the animal species are very different in India compared to those in Scotland and therefore need to be described with different terms. So far English has been able to remain relatively coherent, but if and when the diversification of the varieties continue, new solutions must be found .

Title: Feminists have done more harm to the cause of women than good

Secondly, if women are willing to play within the male capitalistic system, for the system to be truly equal it would have to go through a fundamental change and men are not willing to let that happen. Today, especially the Scandinavian countries brag about their being equal societies when, in fact, they are far from it. If one takes a critical look at women's working conditions, one sees that they still have to work twice as hard as men for a same salary; if they are lucky enough to get a same salary as men. Also, the home is still very much in the women's responsibility and for the work they do there they get neither money nor proper recognition. The reason why homework has never been defined as ' proper ' work is the fact that it is not ' productive', whatever that then means. Women just raise their kids, keep the house clean and food on the table, and all that for free. Men's precious little capitalistic system would be destroyed if all of a sudden all that voluntary labor would have to paid for. The ' male as a provider of the family ' myth has successfully prevented a true equality with women's right to their own money to take place. If we continued to look at the bigger frame of our ' equal ' society, we would see how the pension system, social welfare and all other safety nets in our society are unequally balanced toward women .

Title: Is reading a book better than watching its movie adaptation?
Paragraph: Many passionate film-lovers wouldn't agree regarding the advantages of books and therefore it is also important to take into account the fact that films, as well, have their advantages. Since the letters on the book papers and covers are "dead" and cannot create real images, films are there to perform the task. Directors would agree that they are actually the ones bringing books into life on silver screens giving their audience a chance to "meet" their beloved characters from the books and perceive them as real people, especially if they are portrayed by their favourite actors. Actions and the plot elements benefit from book adaptations since they can be adapted successfully in a manner which is not time-consuming as the book is due to time-limitations of films which usually last from hour and a half to three hours. But what about scholars? Films are beneficial for them just as books are. Due to visual stimuli, they can easily associate new vocabulary, even grammar patterns with their favourite scenes and memorize them even more effectively. Plus, in films, the chances are that they would become familiar with everyday language compared to more formal structures in books. Yet, we should keep in mind that, for example, Twilight saga films are the highest grossing vampire films of all times with combined taking of more than three billion dollars, whereas only saga's fifth part earned 340 million dollars, which is a proof of popularity of films. Thirdly books are rather unjustifiably seen as tedious and boring, demanding a lot of time, which is partially true since film can convey the same experience of the book in even less than two hours, which is important in our fast modern age.

Title: The prison system is outdated. No civilized society should punish its criminals: it should rehabilitate them

Such a man often does not find any job because of his past as a criminal and because of his lack of experience; a criminal who has spent a long time in prison is most of the time completely out of his depth when confronted to the new situation. That is why he very soon becomes an outcast. The only way which remains open to him is thus to fall back into criminality. It is moreover well-known that during their incarceration, people often meet not very recommendable criminals who can have a bad influence on them and sometimes can ' help ' them to enter a well-organized criminal network. Title: Can alternative energy replace fossil fuels?
Paragraph: Both sides offer up equally compelling arguments, and despite all the back and forth bickering, there is a reasonable compromise on the horizon which would address the concerns of both parties in a reasonable manner. It is evident that the main point of contention regarding the issue is that of money, be it the economy itself or corporate profits. Carefully inching closer towards renewable energy and encouraging its growth via subsidies would give a leg up to the fledgling industry, creating much-needed competition for the fossil fuel monopoly. This kind of healthy competition is welcome in every economy, to provide alternatives to citizens, which would in turn play the two rivals further and further against each other, stimulating growth of the GDP. A slower approach might actually end up being a boon for both sides, as it would also give a chance to fossil fuel companies to perhaps diversify or reestablish themselves entirely in the new industry hailed by many to be the future of the energy sector. Last but not least, any implementation of renewable energy comes with an added bonus: a greener, healthier Earth. Fossil fuels may have been a necessary evil in the past, but now it is finally time to put some distance between us and them .

Title: Should Animal Testing Be Banned
Paragraph: On the other hand, the protectors of animals as living beings have come up with their own set of arguments to counter-pose the statements made by animal testing promoters. The first argument that firmly asserts the contributions to medical causes has been contradicted by the Humane Society International, which provides readily available examples of animal cruelty, mostly leading up to premature death. Such cases of exerting force on animals for testing purposes include prolonged periods of physical restraints, forced inhalation and feeding, food and water deprivation, leading up to the commonly used LD50 (lethal dose 50) test which is conducted in order to pinpoint the precise dose of a certain chemical which would kill 50% of the animals tested. As for the second argument, the con-camp used offensive measures to ensure best defense and came up with a line of alternatives to testing on living animals. Not only do they promote in vitro testing (cell cultures in a petri dish) which allows for human cells to be tested for better results, but they also encourage microdosing (administration of extra small doses that do not cause adverse effects) on human volunteers. Plus, a sustainable solution had been offered to skin product companies enabling them to test their products on human skin cells grown in tubes which are already available on the market (like EpiDerm). Ultimately, when reaching the third argument that promotes animal testing, researchers and animal rights activists do not fail to distribute just the right dosage of argumentative antidote. Consequently, they acknowledge the beneficial effects to animal disease treatments, but underline the damage made by those chemicals that are harmful to animals. Namely, the effects may vary when it comes to human organisms, causing detrimental loss of precious remedies solely due to the fact that it turned out badly for the rats in the experiment. Cases of existing cures which have malfunctioned during experiments on animals are Aspirin and Fk-506 (tacrolimus), a chemical substance which lowers the risk of organ transplant rejection. Hence, the protectors of animal rights manage to use an everyday medicine in order to serve their point when it comes to human suffering at the expense of misleading experimental results from animals.

Title: Foreigners are no longer welcome in Germany
Paragraph: This opinion seems to be in many minds nowadays, but I'm absolutely convinced that the main part of Germans don't think that way. They all bring a part of their culture with them, not only by opening restaurants, but also by arranging folcloristic and ethnic events and parties. I remember one which took place last week in Marktoberdorf. About 200 Asylum seekers from Africa or India, who have to live in shanty and ugly barracks, had organized an open-air-concert, where they performed ethnic dances or typical African music like Reggae. Although the weather hadn't been too fine and heavy thunderstorms were forecasted, there came more than one thousand visiters from allover the Ostallgäu, and I'm sure they all were fascinated by this enourmous spectacle of sound, light and food. The foreigners offered Indian and African foodlike, chicken in curry-sauce or Döner Kebab. The whole spectacle had been an enormous success, and I'm sure that it was a great cultural enrichment for the town and even the whole land. No doubt, lots of people changed their minds about foreigners in Germany. But there are still some people who hold on to their prejudices about foreigners: they are considered to be dirty, criminal, violent, lazy, simply "different" from us Germans. Books like "Nicht ohne meine Tochter", which show a moslem husband, who beats his wife and his daughter, who doesn't allow her to leave the house, who forces her to have sex with him, support such prejudices, and this book had been a bestseller in Germany for nearly one year .

Paragraph: Those major problems notwithstanding, the greatest difficulty in reuniting East and Western Germany however seems to be a psychological one: The one German people, though being reunited, does not seem to be unified at all. When last week an about 35-year-old woman moaned about the higher prices and the expected tax growth because of the unification at the cash desk of a supermarket round the corner, I once more felt that we are far from being an united country. It seems that people have to become aware of the responsibility connected with the reunification and of the need to share first before the actual economic, environmental and social problems in the Eastern parts of the country can be solved. More than a year ago, people were separated although they were only a stone's throw away from each other. Now that the stone walls are removed, it is time that the misgivings and the walls within ourselves start to fall .

Title: Feminists have done more harm to the cause of women than good
Paragraph: In addition, in our modern society women do not pocess the role they used to. They are allowed to do whatever they want, they can go to universities, decide whether marry or not and finally they are considered to be clever enough to compete with a man. But even though those changes have accured we cannot admit that we do not still live in a somewhat patriarchal society. We can admit that women are in a better position than they were in past times but when nowadays a woman is married and also working, she is obliged by the unspoken rule to do on her own the housework and to raise the children without her husband's help .

Title: The story of America
Paragraph: Similarly, large numbers of African-Americans have had to conform to rules totally set from outside. Early in the history of the United States the Southern States based their agriculture on slavery. Slaves, whose number far exceeded the number of the white population, were cheap and bulky labour force. Though slavery was abolished in the course of the American Civil War racial problems have not disappeared. According to studies carried out some years ago, coloured and white Americans largely still lived in separate areas. Accordingly, if a black family, no matter how well to do, moved to a white neighbourhood, the white families usually started to move out one by one, so that the area finally turned to one of coloured people. Another example of the discrimination is that even now there are no mixed couples in American television programs. It can thus be stated that various forms of segregation still prevail in the American society. Furthermore, there are more social problems among the black population than the white, unemployment rates are higher, and there is more crime. Obviously all the blame can not be put on the individuals, there must be something wrong with the circumstances, too. The higher rates of crime and other kind of misbehavior are partly due to hopelessness that in turn follows from the poorer living conditions, quality of life and future prospects of a very high number of a non-white population .

Title: Astrology
Paragraph: Astrology means the art of observing the position of the stars in the belief that they influence human affairs. Astrology started to develop in antiquity, flourished in the Middle Ages. Now it is very often neglected and treated as a pseudo-science. It was astrology that helped astronomy to develop as priests in ancient Mesopotamia and Egypt were so much interested in observing the stars and planets. Nowadays astrology has been using what astronomers have learnt about the heavenly bodies .

Title: One should never judge a person by external appearances.
Paragraph: The first reason why people should not be judged according to the way they look is based on the emotional level. For example, people wearing dark clothes are often thought to be antisocial, quiet, weird and even suicidal. Other people may be full of prejudices towards them for they are scared of the things with which they are not familiar. They will tend to avoid them and ignore them. But, in most of the cases the truth is somewhat different. These antisocial, unfriendly and unapproachable individuals are actually very cheerful, optimistic and smiling all the time. They do not think bad about life, but tend to use its full maximum instead. The same goes for those looking like junkies. They might just be hard working and responsible like any other person that is considered to be normal according to the standards of our prejudiced society. According to these examples, one should never be judged by their physical appearance taking in account emotional reasons .

Title: How can we keep peace?
Paragraph: Finally, we need to be interested in what's really happening in the area. Many young people in Japan ate not interested in what's happening there at all. Title: Is freedom of speech an illusion?
Paragraph: Freedom of speech is lauded as one of the pillars of western society in the 21st century. It's a basic human right, as proclaimed by United Nations. By rights, the very concept of someone being punished for voicing an opinion should be completely unfathomable in this day and age. However, this is also the age of ubiquitous migration and multiculturalism. The milennial society is a melting pot of different cultures and ethnicities, where any number of international, interracial and interreligious relationships are forged every single day. In order for this model to thrive, mutual respect is paramount. The problem with that is that embracing the new and the unknown is simply contrary to human nature. When presented with something different from the status quo, most humans will react by lashing out. Which means that obliterating discrimination and hate speech is not optional .

Title: Personal Computers
Paragraph: I started to learn to use personal computer when I was a high school students. At that time, only a few high school students could use personal computers. Now I'm writing this essay with the computer in a train and it's very useful. I want to write about the need for everyone to have one very small computer in the future in this essay. It's something like my dream but if it will be realized, the world will be much more comfortable .

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?
Paragraph: Through rehabilitation, prisoners especially those who have been convicted for stealing can learn a decent profession through which they can make a living and avoid committing another felony. Spending six months, one year or several years in jail prevents prisoners from improving their faculties. Some prisoners would not have committed a crime, if they could have made money. As a result, providing some workshops in different fields in the form of rehabilitation system can be a great opportunity for almost all convicts. They can learn a profession and also make money while serving their sentence and save it for the time they are freed. Some individuals are concerned about the dangers of money possession by prisoners. They believe there is a possibility that they use their money to bribe the system authorities or provide some facilities and flee. The solution for this potential problem is quite simple. Offenders can have a bank account and the money can be deposited in this account and they can access the money only after the end of their sentence period. Therefore, rehabilitation can be a very good substitute for imprisonment, since it can improve convicts' lives during sentence period and after freedom.

Title: Abortion

Maybe, they think that it is a part of it's mother body. I think their thought is not surprising but normal as it is within the pregnant woman's body. They may believe that it is entirely a part of her body but if we think in such a way, abortion should not be applied. For some reasons, it can be necessary. For example: In rape and incest situations

Title: Some people say that in our modern world, dominated by science and technology and industrialisation, there is no longer a place for dreaming and imagination. What is your opinion?

Title: Sex Education should Become Obligatory in Schools

Their children's worries and confusion about certain things may seem funny, not serious and not worth mentioning to them. Meanwhile, teenagers are utterly embarrassed and ashamed when they are to discuss sex and other equally tickish issues with their mothers and fathers. They do not know if they can count upon the parents' understanding and discretion or rather expect some critical remarks and false imputations. A school, thus, appears to be an ideal institution where such an education could take place. Young people are among friends and acquaintances which share not only common interests and hobbies but also problems connected with adolescence. Title: Most university degrees are theoretical and do not prepare students for the real world. They are therefore of very little value

Title: Sex Education should Become Obligatory in Schools
Paragraph: Many people object to the plan of introducing sex education as a subject in schools arguing that it is going to serve solely as an instruction lesson of how to properly carry out sexual intercourses. In fact, sex education is far more than merely acquainting young people with a practical side of erotic affection. Primarily, it aims at providing pupils with some intelligible and credible information concerning psychological and physiological development of both sexes. This programmed instruction focuses, in other words, on making young people aware of the current changes occurring in their psyche and body and on teaching them how to accept these processes without any inhibitions. Additionally, the classes attempt to make the everyday social contacts with members of opposite sex easier and less stressful for students. They also emphasise the significance of different human relations like love or friendship and help to get to know and accept young people's most personal feelings and emotions. Thereby, sex education due to its overall approach towards the subject supplies the students with basic and necessary knowledge about sex. It does not at all treat it as the ultimate object of study but rather places it in an appropriate context of such important correlations as maturity and social responsibility .

Title: Those who envision a future world speaking only one tongue, whether English, German, Russian or any other, hold a misguided ideal and would do the evolution of the human mind the greatest disservice. Western culture has made, through language, a provisional analysis of reality and, without correctives, hold resolutely to that analysis as final. The only correctives lie in all those other tongues, which by aeons of independent evolution have arrived at different, but equally logical, provisional analyses. (B. L. Whorf)
Paragraph: First of all, any society possesses its predominant language which carries various attitudes and perceptions of that particular society and which may also be unique and exist solely in that society. For example, in one of his articles the linguist Benjamin Lee Whorf claimed that the Hopi language expresses their understanding of the world and their analysis of reality which differ from Western understanding. Conceptual frameworks, or metaphysics as Whorf calls it, are revealed in the very languages predominant in these cultures. Moreover, Whorf explains that in Western culture, e.g. the English language, people employ the concepts of space and time to make a distinction between static and continuously flowing time as well as to distinguish among the past, present and future. At the meantime, the Hopi language does not make a distinction between the past, present and future even though they employ the other two metaphysical concepts - objective and subjective - to express their understanding of time. The first one encompasses what in Western culture is understood as the past and present, and the latter expresses future and everything a person hopes or believes to happen. These different understandings of reality are manifested in the languages themselves, i.e. formation of sentences and the very words themselves. Despite the fact that the English and the Hopi languages are not isolated one from another and have some contact points, the two cultures hold to their own metaphysics both of which are equally logical and plausible .

Title: Christmas - the best time of the year?
Paragraph: In my family Christmas takes a lazy start at the first Advent Sunday when the children can start opening their Christmas calenders and we light the first of the four Advent candles. From this day forward the children wait anxiously for Christmas Eve; we parents try to make preparations at a slow pace. Fortunately, in my family we traditionally celebrate Christmas at somebody's house gathering all the families together. Usually we start planning Christmas already way back in the summer by planning to whom we'll go, what we'll eat, and so on. We are then six to ten adults and about eight children celebrating the Christmas season together. Each family prepares one of the dishes for the meal plus a variety of cakes and candies. This simplifies the preparations enormously; the mothers will not become totally exhausted .

Title: Animal Testing

There are many alternatives to animal testing, which are as accurate and they should be used instead. These alternatives include tissue cultures and computer modelling. Computer models can be used to model the effects of a compound on a disease and tissue cultures can be used to test the toxicity of a drug. If more of these methods were used, less animal testing would be required .

Title: Do University Degrees prepare students for the real world?
Paragraph: Moreover, our educational system lacks something crucial to our lives in the real world - practice. We do not have the necessary practical experience once we get our university degree, which makes it all the more difficult for us to cope with the "real world" situations once they occur. Therefore, when we obtain a university degree, we do have an academic experience, which, according to most of the employers, is not enough in most of the cases. When we go out looking for a job, it is highly unlikely that we will get one in the field of our choosing solely based on our university degree, having in mind that a university degree does not equal experience in practice, and most employers do not have the time or are not willing to be our tutors - time is money, after all. What we need in order to get a job is the practical, rather than theoretical ' know - how', and since we lack that, we have a lot of difficulty finding a job that is not only well - paid, but is also provides us with both personal and professional growth and a well being in general. In such a time of global crisis, we then begin working for a lot less than we should, and commonly, end up in a field of work different of what our university degree is aimed at. Thus, the overall price of the product drops, and, as a professional, we tend to feel that our product is not valued enough, which makes us frustrated and leaves us wondering "was it all worth it". We are left motiveless, unwilling to continue learning and unwilling to provide for our profession .

Title: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of recycling as a method of waste management in Hong Kong

The greatest box - office hit of the second part of the twentieth century is a film totally shot with the help of advanced technologies , but supported vehemently by imagination. Another instance when technologies cannot do without imaginative power is the sale of new technological products. Title: Some scientists predict that life can be radically prolonged. Would you find that desirable? Explain

Title: The grass looks always greener on the other side.
Paragraph: All in all, people nowadays do not have to worry about how to stay alive, which creates room for worrying about other, material things. Often this dissatisfaction leads to these people's own unhappiness and the grass on the other side does not appear to be greener, but appears to be withered. When people are dying or looking back at (a certain period in) their lives, they realize that though the grass might have appeared to be greener on the other side of the hill, this other side also sloped down to an all-time low. The only instance in which the grass actually is greener on the other side is - ironically - the place where people are six feet under or rather: pushing up daisies .

Title: Some people say that in our modern world, dominated by science technology and industrialisation, there is no longer a place for dreaming and imagination. What is your opinion?

Title: Marx once said that religion was the opium of the masses. If he were alive at the end of the 20th century, he would replace religion with television.
Paragraph: An intense light in the shape of a tunnel is connecting past and present. An austere 19th century figure is being transported from the former to the latter. It is Karl Marx, the German materialistic philosopher, who arrives in a late 20th century average home. In the living room, instead of talking, preferably on social and political issues, that is, instead of having an engaged conversation, he would claim, the family is assembled around a weird retangular shinning object in which moving figures were displayed. No one utters a single word. They do not even exchange a unique glance. All together and, yet, each on their own. What impression would Karl Marx have on so common a scene in our time? A man whose philosophical thought focused on society and mass organization would definitely not figure out what kind of group association the picture described above potrayed. He is then told about what is happening and, based on the information he was given, he comes to the conclusion that, besides religion, television, the name given to the strange gadget, is the opium of the masses .

Title: A defence of examinations
Paragraph: Education specialists and psychologists maintain that examinations increase school anxiety, weigh heavily on weak pupils and create a kind of pressure that blows the desire to learn like an autumn gale out of the head of even the most curious child. There is no doubt a grain of truth in this argument, but speaking honestly, who can claim that he would have studied hard without being put under constant pressure? The idea of mankind living happy and healthy with everybody working according to his capacities and being remunerated according to his needs has proved a gross error of history. With exams it is just the same - we need pressure in our student lives as well as in our working lives if we want to succeed. Granting to education specialists that this is a thing called "extrinsic motivation" and compares most unfavourably with the real inherent thirst for knowledge, it is a myth to believe that we could do just as well without .

Title: In the 21st century the aural/visual media will be far more important than the written word

Although combinations of aural and visual media and writing will become even more common than they are now, (for example cd-rom books and encyclopaedias) writing is still included in these, and is likely to be in the 21st century as well, although perhaps to a lesser extent than now. Writing also plays an important part in all companies and institutions, and will, due to the economical nature of it, continue to do so. All writing cannot be easily converted into film, for example, and although it can be read and stored as aural information, and shared by aural media, it loses much in terms of accessibility. A written text provides the best possibilities to store detailed and complex information, and this is not likely to change although the use of other media will increase greatly over the next few years or decades .

Title: More donors, please!
Paragraph: Monday 24 April 1995, University Hospital. A young girl was brought in this morning with serious heart complaints. After carrying out all necessary examinations the doctors come to the awful conclusion that the little girl can only stay alive with a heart transplant. The only thing they can do now is wait for a donor. Three days later, Julie, who was only seven years old, dies. People are dying under these circumstances every day. But of all those deaths, there are many that could have been prevented. Many people are on a waiting list for an organ transplant, hoping that, one day, there might be a deceased person who carries a donor card and thus can save their lives by giving them the organs they need. However, people often have to wait too long and many of them die while still in the waiting list. Apparently, the number of organs that are needed is much bigger than the number of organs that are offered. Evidently, the current donor card situation does not lead to a sufficient supply of donors. Therefore, it might be advisable to reverse the current donor card situation and thus make people automatic donors, unless their donor card says otherwise .

Title: Some people say that in our modern world, dominated by science and technology and industrialisation, there is no longer a place for dreaming and imagination. What is your opinion?
Paragraph: There are various other factors that limit the development of our mind. First, the communication development, as it has become so easy, there is less need to concentrate the mind. To receive information, we need only sit in front of the TV set and flick from channel to channel with a remote control. To impart information, we need only pick up a telephone and throw out phrases in random sequence, punctuated with "ums" and "errs". In the 60s the Canadian philosopher Marshall McLuhan predicted the advent of film and TV would have as radical an effect on our culture as the invention of a printing press had on the culture of the Middle Ages. Title: What is the best literature for your child?
Paragraph: Two shy young girls, of about twelve years of age, come into the book-shop. They immediately go up to the saleswoman and ask her if she has any sad stories that have actually happened. The lady walks them to the section of books that is full of sorrow and distress. Because the number of children has grown tremendously over the last twenty-five years, also he number of children's books that are offered has increased tenfold. Every year approximately twelve hundred new books came into the market. In the last few years, over three and a half million books were sold, which represent a market-value of fifty million guilders. This is only a very small amount compared to the one billion guilders that are spent on toys on a yearly basis. Even though the figure of 3.5 million books may seem quite large, children should be encouraged to read more. Almost 75 % of the Dutch population believe that a book is more important for a child's education than a computer, a TV set, a newspaper or a museum. But the questions then is: what should your child read? Is it in your child's best interest to give him only books which have literary merit, or should you let him read whatever he wants? This essay will argue that the best thing is for children to choose the books they like, regardless of their literary value. First of all, it will be good to present some of the reasons of why it is important for adults to read. Second, it will be argued that in order for people to acquire a taste for reading, it is important that they should start reading at a very early age, before the age that they even learn to read. Thirdly, it will be pointed out how important reading is for a child, and a comparison will be made between the ways in which a child chooses a book and the ways in which an adult chooses a book. The last point will elucidate the fact that books of ' low ' literary value are actually not harmful for your child at all. Finally a conclusion wil be reached in the last paragraph .

Furthermore, the adopted child will have proper education, health care and a right to accomplish his own interests and dreams like every other child. Title: English is increasingly becoming a world language. Why do you think this is so? What should  be the characteristics of a world language?
Paragraph: The English language began spreading from England first to the nearby areas and later around the world. This was from the late Middle Ages onwards, when Britain started creating colonies. The language has spread to the whole of North America and to large areas in Africa and Asia due to colonialism. Still, there are other ways of a language spreading, too. One is the spread by migration. For example the large numbers of the Irish immigrating to USA in the 19th century. Yet another type of a language spread is that without a serious population spread, that is through exchanging scientific, technological and cultural information. As examples of English dominating in this area Crystal gives scientific writing, academic conferences, diplomacy, airports, air-traffic control, international competitions, pop music and advertising among others . <R> The reasons for English and not some other language being the world language of today are numerous. Crystal argues that the most important one is the economical power of the United States . <R> It is undoubtedly one of the central factors but since the end of the 1980s there has occurred a shift in the world politics following the breaking up of the Soviet Union. The United States is clearly the dominating world power today. The language spoken there has thus become even more dominant, too .

Title: However charged language is abused and whatever misunderstandings it may cause, we still have to live with it—and even by it. (…) The relatively uncharged language of bare factual statement, though it serves its informative purpose well and is much less open to abuse and misunderstanding, can describe only the bare land of factual knowledge; to communicate knowledge of the turbulences and the calm and the deep currents of the sea of inner experience we must use charged language.(Newman P. Birk and Genevieve B. Birk)
Paragraph: To realize this means to see the reasons why it is so. What lies at the core of our life is actually language. Newman P. Birk and Genevieve B. Birk say that regardless of the effects language has upon us, <*> ; I would go as far as to say that to a certain extent we have to live in it. The Modernist concept of language suggests that language does not really offer a window on the world but actually it constructs the world in which we live. This is especially true of mass media-related realities for they are one of the best sites in which charged language can be observed to construct the world. Therefore, even phrases such as "bare factual statement" should be looked at with a grain of salt. It is always worthwhile to remember that what appears to be straightforward statements of fact are often products of a conscious attempt to make someone buy into something. Language thus always serves the purposes of those who use it and the degree to which it is charged is dependent upon those purposes .

Title: Those who envision a future world speaking only one tongue, whether English, German, Russian or any other, hold a misguided ideal and would do the evolution of the human mind the greatest disservice. Title: Should higher education be free?
Paragraph: First of all, I think higher education should be free because it would ease the financial strain put on students and their parents, while at the same time encouraging more people to continue their education and develop proficiency in a field that will ultimately broaden their professional horizon. Costly tuition fees are often the make or break in one's decision to either attend university or not. This means that prospective students have to take up jobs to pay for their tuition, take up a student loan and pay it back after they graduate, or seek financial support from their families. Whatever the option, the costs of studying which include accommodation, books, supplies, and travel fees can put one in quite the financial predicament. However, if higher education were to be free, students would concentrate more on their studies rather than worry about how to make ends meet each month. Furthermore, higher education should be free because it would help people find employment much easier as demands for university degrees as a job prerequisite have become more and more common in job advertisements. Even jobs that previously have been filled by high school graduates now seem to look for candidates with more formal training. In fact, high school certificates seem to have lost all their merit in modern society and have become a basic reflection of one's education and do not do high school graduates any favors post-graduation. Society has slowly transitioned into a society of degrees. In this regard, the cost of higher education may hinder the professional playing field. Consequently, perhaps only those who can afford the cost can get a degree, while those who may also have the will and skill to complete a university course cannot afford to go and as a result they will not be on the same level as their counterpart with a degree. Therefore, it appears that in order to secure a respected well-paying job and level the professional playing field, attaining a university degree is the way to go. If this is the reality members of society must face, then they should at least be given the right to free higher education. My final argument as to why higher education should be free and accessible to all is because it would promote a more educated and open-minded society and decrease poverty and criminal activities.

Title: Children with Down's Syndrome should not be put away

Specialists already know several important facts about Down Syndrome, but they are not able to get into it too far. Scientists announced that this disease is caused at the very early stage of the development by false cell division when one additional chromosome is produced. Of much importance is also the age of a future mother. It was proved that the older mother is the greater possibility of her child to suffer from "mongolism". In the past specialists also advised parents to get rid of such children maintaining that it would be completely impossible to keep them at home. It is a wonderful thing that people who have dealt with that problem decided to change this attitude and show the world that retarded children are "also" human and that they should live human life. Children with Down Syndrome are not a burden for their families as they were thought to be in the past. It is quite the opposite. Title: Genetic manipulation
Paragraph: There are many dangers involved in genetic manipulation. All living things can be genetically manipulated, including the microscopic forms of life which are our biggest enemies, the organisms that transmit diseases. Just as the atomic bomb was invented to keep the peace, so new diseases are created for biological warfare in the name of peace. There are strong indications that the USA have already used such microscopic weapons in Vietnam. There is some evidence that the emergence of a new strand of the bubonic plague, which died out quickly due to an unexpected lack of hosts (fleas), had been introduced by the US army. Even AIDS might well have been an experiment that got out of control. The possibility exists that a genetically manipulated disease, which would have no natural cure, escapes from a laboratory by accident, or is used in war and thus become a threat to a large part of the world population .

Title: The prison system is outdated. No civilized society should punish its criminals: it should rehabilitate them
Paragraph: On the other hand, habitual criminals and people found guilty of major crimes like murder, rape or drug smuggling, deserve no merciful treatment. Their grave actions justify grave reactions from the authorities. In order for the purposes of punishment to be successful, the penalty has to be in accordance with the severity of the crime. This is especially important to prevent future crimes, and to serve the general public's sense of justice. How can future delinquents be discouraged from embarking upon a criminal career if they don't fear the consequences it will entail? How can the average citizen feel safety for his life and property if people who transgress against him aren't punished? With a system like this, citizens will take matters into their own hands, arm themselves and form volatile vigilantes for self-protection, with the subsequent risk of random shoot-outs and killings. To prevent such conditions and emphasise that it is indeed the government who is in charge of upholding the law, society needs to meet major crimes with major punishments, including the possibility of lifetime imprisonment, and - in extreme cases - capital punishment .

Title: The functions of a university

Universities have changed a lot during their history. They function in a world substantially different from bygone times, during which the pace of change was slow and university education was restricted to a small elite with a common cultural background. In the expanded world community of our time the university is no longer an ivory tower. The modern universities are subjected both to internal forces of traditional educational values and objectives and to external forces of ' customer ' demand. There should be a balance between these forces. Universitie's major consideration should be tomorrow's world. Such an orientation does not mean abandonment of the traditional values of Western civilization and the traditional functions of the universities, but a broadening of scholarly interests into the direction of future's demands .

Title: Detailed Definition of the Term 'Intelligence'
Paragraph: Secondly intelligence requires reasoning. So for example, a student of English Philology must develop various strategies of reasoning in order to acquire knowledge in many subjects and on many levels of abstraction. The reasoning processes include an ability to deduct, that is to reason from general principles to a particular case. So a student of English, while learning phonological principles about aspiration, on the basis of general rule should be able to say which sounds in English are aspirated and which are not. The opposite mental process of reasoning is called induction, which involves discovering general laws from particular facts or examples. Thus, a student of English given only various instances of words in which sounds are aspirated, should be able to come up with a general principle referring to the process of aspiration in English language. Moreover, intelligence denotes an ability to synthetise, that is to combine separate facts or parts into one unity. This ability is necessery in learning for example history, because a student has to learn many historical events in order to have a general "picture" of history. The opposite mental process is connected with analysing things to study their structure. This type of thinking which demands careful analysis to understand mathaphors and symbols is necessary in studying for example literature .

Title: Does technology make us more alone?

More precisely, they will tend to feel more relaxed and open by using specific devises like social networks or mobile phones to convey their thoughts and feelings, by posting some pictures or writing just a few simple sentences. Title: Water
Paragraph: Not only does the total amount of fresh water decreases, so does its quality. The pollution of groundwater appears to be a world-wide problem caused by, amongst others, the drainage of waste water and the usage of pesticides. Furthermore, through leaking pipelines toxic substances end up in both ground - and surfacewater. Besides, the limited fresh watersupply is threatened by major environmental disasters, like the nuclear explosion in Tsjernobil, and by the increasing worldpopulation. The shortage of fresh water will be so serious, that scientists predict that already in the next century fresh water supplies will be depleted to such an extent that forty to fifty per cent of the world population will have to deal with a grave shortage of water. Fresh water will become the new precious and expensive export product from countries like Norway, Alaska and Greenland. It will be so scarce that future wars will no longer be about matters such as territory or petroleum, but about the possesion of fresh water. The past already proved that the possesion of sweet water could be worth a war. Boetros Ghali, the former vice president of Egypt, regulary threatened his neighbouring countries that every attempt to interfere with the water level of the Nile would be interpreted as a threat to the national security of his country and, therefore, could end in a war .

Even today all branches of society have their own misfortunate ones, but ethnic minorities and the disabled are the most visible to us. The equally visible winners are healthy, young, white men. Especially when it comes to business, hiring and firing, we see social Darwinism at its worst. The most attractive worker is a well educated white man in his early 30s. Other groups, like women, coloured or even people less beautiful than average are being left outside the elite circle. Also in politics the arena belongs mostly to the white male. The traditional male roles in society are so well imprinted in people's minds that for a long time nobody questioned them. Are women afraid of power? Are coloured people afraid of speaking to the press? Does a person in a wheelchair do a poorer job than a healthy person? The truth could be that white men simply are better suited for politics and business than others, but there is just too much evidence arguing against this. With the right background you are fit for all sorts of work, but only a small percentage of the world's population have the opportunities offered to almost every white man. If you want to learn a foreign language, it can also help you a lot to read a book which is not in your mother's tongue. Books can also teach you good behaviour. You can learn from the characters what kind of behaviour is good or awarded and socially accepted and what kind is not. You can see the consequences of different kinds of behaviour or actions. That is something you also have in movies, but the point with the books is that it gives you a lot of details about the character's thoughts about what he or she did. You can also learn how to react in certain situations. Reading about how different characters react differently in some situations, can help you improve yourself. So, as we saw, books are very rife resources of education .

Title: The prison system is outdated. No civilised society should punish its criminals: it should rehabilitate them

Title: Courteous Styles and Informal Styles

However, when I sit for a job interview with a Japanese country, I will use a courteous style because the interviewer and I are not friends. Therefore, we need to think what styles are appropriate in every situation. Finally, I must practice talking in courteous styles for a job interview. In addition, I will belong some company or organization some day, and when I talk with my superiors, I must use the formal styles. Therefore, I want to learn to talk fluently in a formal or courteous style .

Title: Vegetarianism should be avoided

Title: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of banning smoking in restaurants
Paragraph: There are many advantages of banning smoking in restaurant. First, all the non-smokers are benefited. The non-smokers usually feel very uncomfortable when they breath in Secondhand smoker, especially those who are allergic to smoke. Breathing Secondhand smoke largely increases that risk of lung cancer and heart disease by approximately 25%. Tobacco-specific carcinogens have been found in the blood and urine of non-smokers who have exposed to environmental tobacco smoke. Smoking in restaurants and bars cause serious ' air-pollution'. To illustrate the effects of this air pollution on restaurant and bar workers, this project studied the association between the environmental tobacco smoke exposure and respiratory symptoms in a cohort of 53 bartenders, before and after California's prohibition on smoking in all bars and taverns. Most of the bartenders during this time. Smoking also destroy the taste of the food felt by citizens, banning smoking can preserve the odour of food, which attract more citizens to come there to have their lunch or dinner. It was concluded that the establishment of smoke-free bars and taverns was associated with improvement of respiratory health .

Title: Pre-Celtic Ireland

Another innovation was the use of water to provide defence for settlements on artificial islands - today known as "crannogs". For the first time, it seems, war and battle became to play a part in the lives of the Irish .

Title: Some people say that in our modern world, dominated by science and technology and industrialisation, there is no longer a place for dreaming and imagination. What is your opinion?
Paragraph: The apect of age may be of great importance as well in connection with dreaming and imagination. We usually say that small children have a wonderful imagination. We are astonished when we see their colourful pictures with purple sun, green sky and yellow trees. It is their complete freedom in thinking that is not tied up with the conventions of grown-up's world. When growing up they learn from the adults that the sun is yelow and the sky is blue and this quality slowly starts to disappear from their pictures. Everything looks very realistic but it is somehow more boring and the adults do not know why. Children are surrounded by a different world than we are. It is a world where we can meet fairies, kings and qeens, gnomes, dragons or talking animals. and where the good always wins over the evil. We regard it as childlish to dwell in this world of imagination longer than you are allowed - i.e. when you are already a grown up. In fact we should be glad to keep this colourful creativity of childhood and we should not be shy to play even in adulthood. When young we usually have great ideals, we dream of what the future would bring, if we do not have enough money we always find a way out using our imagination. If we compare the life to a packet of playing cards, imagination would stand for Joly Joker - you can replace almost anything with it. Young people usually have an uncompromising attitude towards the consumer way of life associated with boreness, conformity and non - invention. Paradoxically the older we grow the more tolerant we are. We are less strict about the ideals we used to have and compromise more often. As the greater part of our lives has passed we dream of how would our lives look like if we did it differently .

Title: Does technology make us more alone?

Title: Should single men/women be allowed to adopt children?
