Is it that big of a difference to raise a child as a couple in contrast to raising the child alone? Well, adopting children is not like getting a dog or a cat. By adopting a child, one is getting their hands on a human being, and single people need to face certain criteria .

Title: Lying is immoral and should always be condemned

Similiar to ill people, children should not be told the whole truth. Their mental development should define the criteria for telling them one things and not the others. It does not mean that children should be isolated from the world full of scenes of violence, brutality and famished people on the streets; nevertheless, kids should not be told about facts that may exert disruptive influence on their minds .

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?
Paragraph: The last and most important flaw that one might find with this method is that the definition of punishment and retribution cannot be found in rehabilitation. Some crimes, such as murder, are too big to go unpunished. In some cultures, even for lesser crimes such as theft, the eye for an eye approach is taken and in addition to returning the stolen property to the rightful owner, the convict would also have to be punished; sometimes savagely and inhumanly. In such cultures, simply rehabilitating the criminals and sending them back to the society would not suffice; they have to be punished accordingly. Now I ask of you, what do you think is the best way to punish someone who has committed an unimaginable crime such as murder? Is it retribution? An eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth, and a life for a life? I, for one, would disagree. Taking one person's life would not return another's. Is it sending the murderer to prison for a long time where he or she would eat good food, have decent close and somewhere to sleep while the tax received from the same people whose loved one is taken away by the said criminals is used to fund the luxuries the murderer would have? This does not seem fair either. Once again I ask of you: what do you think is the proper punishment for such a person?

Title: Whether Finnish schools should teach many languages to a lower level or few languages to a higher standard - which languages and why.

I know many pupils who would like to study many more languages than what is possible today. The amount of teaching in years is irrelevant. I believe that as long as the children have a good basic knowledge of English, a solid rock with what they always feel secure and comfortable, they can learn and want to learn other foreign languages. I, for example, know three or four languages well, but I can also quite easily improve my skills in those other languages, because I have once studied them. As I also consider the communicative skills to be more important than the knowledge of grammar, one should emphasize different communicative acts in teaching, thus gaining those basic needs that are required in the life of business, whether we decide to join the big European family or not .

Title: The enormous variation in individuals on the basis of differences in age, sex, intelligence and personality means that any concept of 'national identity' is meaningless. Do you agree?
Paragraph: There are certain characteristics that usually form a group of people into one nation. There is the territory, on which they live and often have lived for thousands of years. There is a common religion that they all, or most of them, confess to. There is a language they all know. There is the same kind of mentality, brought out by the heritage and the environment. Created by these factors, there is the culture they all have in common; traditions they share and a way of thinking. Some of these characteristics, or all, shape this group of individuals into a people. In many cases, these common features have existed for so long that outsiders can clearly see this group as distinct from others in behaviour, looks and mentality .

Title: Lying is immoral and should always be condemned

In such a situation, a doctor has every right to hide the truth in order not to deprive his/her patient of the last hope and add to his/her suffering. Title: However charged language is abused and whatever misunderstandings it may cause, we still have to live with it—and even by it. (…) The relatively uncharged language of bare factual statement, though it serves its informative purpose well and is much less open to abuse and misunderstanding, can describe only the bare land of factual knowledge; to communicate knowledge of the turbulences and the calm and the deep currents of the sea of inner experience we must use charged language.(Newman P. Birk and Genevieve B. Birk)
Paragraph: The author's talk on language is not quite straightforward. Even though at first sight it seems that they try to underscore the marked contrast between charged and uncharged language and notions attached to the two types, at a closer examination it becomes increasingly obvious that it is not really the case. What they actually talk about is "The relatively uncharged language of bare factual statement" and charged language of "the sea of inner experience". Therefore, the idea of uncharged language is an illusion and a contradiction in terms to a smaller or greater extent, language is always charged in some way .

Title: Cities Take Over

Title: Should cannabis be legalized for medical purposes?
Paragraph: Furthermore, as opposed to other types of treatment and medication, marijuana is less harmful and causes less damage to the body. While doing research, a team of scientists discovered that chemotherapy results in damaging the healthy body cells and they consequently release a protein that causes tumor growth <R> . The researchers found out that this protein, also known as WNT16B, increases cancer cell survival, and that is the reason why chemotherapy ends lives faster <R> . According to Dr. Weil <R> , cannabis is very helpful for cancer patients and it is less toxic than other medicinal drugs which are currently in use. An article written by Patients for Medical Cannabis <R> explains that when comparing the side-effects when using aspirin vs. while using marijuana, the results are in favour of marijuana as a type of medication that is less dangerous for the body. When patients use cannabis, it can result in a short-term memory loss, euphoria, forgetfulness, paranoia and even depression, but all of these side-effects do not persist when marijuana wears off. That is not the case with aspirin. Further in the article, Patients for Medical Cannabis <R> states that consumption of aspirin can cause nausea, abdominal pain, lethargy, as well as serious conditions such as cerebral edema, which is water accumulation on the brain, and irreversible liver damage .

Title: Should teachers rely on modern technological innovations in the teaching process?

Title: The prison system is outdated. No civilized society should punish its criminals: it should rehabilitate them

The starting point would then be to use qualified personnel, especially psychologists who would evaluate them and decide what line of action should be followed in each case. The guards should also be selected according to the same criterias, since it has been proved that they play an important part in the corruption within such institutions. The next step would be to encourage the convicts to develop their interests and hobbies, that means, to help them improve their minds and personnality. A school-like prison, with courses of all kinds, would be, according to me, the most intelligent and efficient method. Education for the uneducated, further learning for the cultivated ones, psychological assistance and last, but not least, constant contacts with the outside world, would lead them to a positive evolution .

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?

Title: Children should be kept on a leash

Speaking from experience, then, I honestly maintain that it is irresponsible to let young children run free and that the only effective way to prevent them from making mischief is to keep them on a leash .

Title: Only an ignorant town dweller can yearn to live on the country!

My impression that they were boring was only confirmed when an agricultural exhibition was held in the village in autumn, and it became obvious that their world was centered around new plough models, the most beautiful ram in the county, and prize-winning pumkin preserves. On top of everything, my friends didn't come to see me any more as often as they used to do during the first months of my new country life, because their enthusiasm for my little cottage and for being chased about by rams when going for walks had cooled off, and so they didn't find it worthwhile anymore to take the fairly long trip to my village. Most of the time I have to go and see them now, if I want to meet them. This means, that I'm going into the city very often now. I have even started doing some parts of my work at the office, although I could do them just as well at home, simply in order to have people around me at least during the day .

Title: The educational system of Lithuania should be reformed: a student's view. Agree or disagree

However, personally I am for this common examination, since I think that any person living in Lithuanian needs to be able to speak perfect Lithuanian, not depending on his nationality or his mother tongue. The thing is that after finishing the school the vast majority of young people enter a university or some college, where they continue their education basically in the Lithuanian language. What is more, if a person intends to stay in Lithuania in future and to live and work in this country, he will soon experience how much he needs a good level of Lithuanian, at least for the reason that a person who speaks Lithuanian with a strong accent or makes mistakes while speaking, has almost no chances to get a good job and to make a good living, since even a waitress is required to speak nice Lithuanian. Besides in my opinion the Lithuanian language examination should be of the same level for all school-leavers for the reason of equality, so that, when entering a university, all young people would be rated according to their marks for the same tasks .

Title: Is the external examination a failed concept?
Paragraph: The external examination is a form of evaluation of pupils in primary (4-9th grade) and secondary schools as well as an evaluation of their teachers <R> . It consists of 30 questions with multiple choice answers and is conducted at the end of each school year. The official reasons for introducing the external examination are verifying the teachers' objectivity in their assessment of the pupils' learning and checking what the pupils have learned during the whole year <R> . While verifying the teachers' objectivity may not sound as a bad idea, the external examination is in all probability not the right method to do it. First of all, one cannot expect a teacher's grade which is a sum of a pupil's knowledge and activity throughout the year, to match the grade of a multiple choice test at the end of the year because the assessment criteria differ greatly. Second, the negative effect it has on teachers is rather serious as the results from the examination might prevent their professional advancement. Another important issue which arises is the range of negative implications for the pupils who will get a failing grade on the external examination and might be unable to pursue further education, especially abroad .

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?

Title: Volunteerism
Paragraph: First, I'd like to make clear what we use our money for. Most of us would think that we buy whatever we want to have, for example, food, housing, clothing and so on. I wonder that if we have a lot of time, do we still buy food? What I means is that if we have nothing else to do except planting vegetables and water them and grow them, do we still need money to have that vegetable? I don't think so, because then we don't need to buy vegetables. We can grow them and can eat them. Even if we have lots of time, do we still need to buy clothing? I don't think so. We can make thread, nit them into clothing, or we can make thread into cloth and cut them so that we can wear it. In this type of living do we need money? I don't think so. Since we have plenty of time, we can work for gaining what we need. In this sense, we can say that we work and make money to buy our time. By paying money, we buy time for us to do something extra. If we make thread and make cloth and cut them into a dress, it takes forever, maybe a whole year. However, if we pay money for a ready-made dress, we can get the dress in 10 seconds and we can use the rest of the time for whatever we'd like to spend. For example, we can use the rest of the time for taking a trip, for watching movies, or for reading books. In summary, we can say that we pay money for buying our time. The picture looks very awkward if we agree to this, because we work hard to make money and spend the money to buy our time. Still in this way, we can do what we'd like to do instead of doing whatever we should do, for example growing vegetables in the dirt .

Title: Gandhian thoughts
Paragraph: A concept of action that explicitly includes training in the "art of dying" in its program, can certainly not be accused of naively or cowardly denying the existence of conflict. However, arguments against Gandhi's precept are readily at hand; First, his words might be regarded as bordering on the pathetic: In our Western political systems the activist can easily practise ahimsa without risking her/his life; Self-suffering including the loss of life also poses a practical problem. If the requirement is so hard, who will be willing to embrace satyagraha? A low number of participants, however, reduces the coercive power of the movement. Third, if the "true" satyagrahis let themselves be killde in a dangerous conflict, they leave behind in leading positions those who might be more willing to adopt violent means. Another dispute arises over the question whether the improvement of society justifies the loss of lives. Can people agree to Gandhi's statement that suffering death for satyagraha "ennobles those who loose their lives and morally enriches the world for their sacrifice" <R> ? Is this enough comfort for the ones who are left behind by their killed loved ones?

Title: Those who envision a future world speaking only one tongue, whether English, German, Russian or any other, hold a misguided ideal and would do the evolution of the human mind the greatest disservice. Title: Should universities be allowed to solve their financial problems by themselves ?
Paragraph: To say nothing more of an issue of the regular, substantial aids which should be granted by the government, on the other hand, the universities should be able to self-finance themselves. Above all, a resolution is needed which would allow the universities to become more autonomous and to eliminate their traditional stiffness, so that they could raise money they are in short of, in great part, by themselves. In a large measure, this could be obtained by levying on students tuition charges. Payments should be required of full-time and part-time students alike. They would have to pay fees and living costs, but every student should be guaranteed to receive from the local authority of the place where he or she lives a personal grant or a loan, high enough to cover their full costs including lodging and food. This would be necessary since in case fees are introduced, many students coming from poor families would not be able to afford studying. As far as loans are concerned, these would have to be payed back in instalments by a student after his or her graduation and taking up regular employment .

Some might argue that learning how to express themselves is also important for students. However, even in present, students have enough freedom to express themselves because they are trying to look attractive through wearing make-up, dying or curling their hair and getting fancy bags or coats. Also, students' fashion can be abused in other way, not just expressing their individuality. In fact, one brand padded jacket was all the craze among students about a year ago, which was so burdensome to most parents. We cannot say that this phenomenon is driven from desire of self-expression or learning how to express themselves, because most students wore the jackets exactly the same. Actually, students' desire to be superior was expressed as showing off their expensive clothes, and conformity effect made the situation more serious. In this circumstance, it would be too premature to liberalize school uniform .

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?

But there is a problem here; that is the ability of proving the kind of the crime from the aspect of the intentionality of the criminal because some crimes are difficult to be proven as incidental or intentional; off course the discussion about this field is not related to our subject and should be investigated somewhere else; however it can be proven that a crime would not be intentional, in my idea the imprisonment should be substituted with a useful punishment that has a result for both sides. In my opinion, punishment means something when a crime is committed intentionally. But when there is not any intention there, it is meaningless. In these situations imprisonment even may result upside down for some reasons like unsuitable conditions of the prisons that these kinds of criminals are not accustomed to them and most of the time the social norms and principles and the health conditions of these persons are deteriorated gradually. But for the compensation of the damaged one/s the criminal should pay something like financial payments or working instead of direct payment and such things are more suitable. Title: What steps might a country take to prevent the spread of the bubonic plague or similar epidemic to that country from abroad ?

Vaccination would have to be compulsory and certificates of inoculation would need to be given so that police, the army and people working for the NHS would be able to check if everybody secured herself or himself from the disease .

Title: Discuss the pros and cons of constructing a second railway link to the mainland

Therefore, the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (SAR) government considers to construct a second railway link to the mainland. It starts from Sheung Shui to Lok Ma Chau. The argument issue has been raised about the second railway will pass through along the Long Valley. It is a rural area without being encroached by city development. Title: Food Crisis

Genetically engineered (GE) food is popular all over the world today. However, there are pros and cons about this issue. In Germany and France, for example, regulators have already banned its use. On the other hand, about sixty percent of the processed food in the United States contains genetically engineered ingredients. The main issue about GE food is its safety. Should the United States have more strict regulations about GE food? Nobody knows what will happen to our health by continually eating GE foods. I strongly suggest that the United States should have strict regulations on these types of foods .

People with these diseases usually have to go through lots of painful treatment, yet cannot be assured of being able to live regular lives again. Title: The prison system is outdated. No civilized society should punish its criminals: it should rehabilitate them
Paragraph: For many years there have been a lot of research on repetitive crime in general, and in connection with this, many convicts have been interviewed about their lives and beings. Based upon these interviews the researchers have been able to draw conclusions about different aspects that need to be discussed in order to be able to change the current situation. First of all it is important to look at the prison system. Many people today have the opinion that the prison system is outdated, and that rehabilitation is better than punishment. To find out why the system is outdated it is necessary to have a look at how the system functioned years back compared to today's system. One hundred years ago imprisonment was completely different from today. The social conditions were horrible. Physical punishment was an everyday occurrence, and the food was often poor with no or little nourishing value. Entertainment of any kind was unthinkable, and the overall conditions were inhuman compared to today's standard. Today convicts live a rather comfortable life with good food, proper clothing, entertainment of various kinds, and access to almost whatever they wish for. The only thing they are denied is their freedom to come and go as they please. First of all, many people believe that convicts today live a luxurious life instead of getting the punishment they deserve in payment for the damage they have caused. Secondly, they claim that the prison system is more or less a hatching nest for criminals. It is also said that if you are not a drug addict the first time you serve a sentence, you will come out being one after a short time. There must be something completely wrong with the security system allowing this to happen .

Title: Is freedom of speech an illusion?
Paragraph: Another factor that "helps" free speech lose its meaning is the fact that most people who use the Internet to express opinions are not aware that their comments lack reasoning and critical analysis. They are often based on selective "truths", generally accepted, but often mistakenly interpreted information and prejudice. Since we are being flooded by vast amount of information on any given topic from the war in Syria to whether or not Leonardo DiCaprio will finally win an Oscar, it is often difficult to extract the most important content that can help us form an opinion worth stating. Sometimes, there is just not enough time to get all the facts, and sometimes having all the fact does not help if we are not capable of interpreting them in the right way. All this leads to people using their "freedom of speech" without realizing that what they are saying is irrelevant or misguided. If we are free to speak and have nothing useful to say, does the freedom in question have meaning? We cannot expect to be taken seriously by our own governments if we do not know how to support our opinions by logic or evidence. It comes as no surprise that so many Europeans are falling victims to hysteria surrounding the latest migration crisis, especially after the Paris attacks. When there is no grounded common-sense opinion, we are nothing but puppets open for all kinds of manipulation .

Title: What is the best literature for your child?
Paragraph: Because children do not learn the alphabet and learn how to read until they are about the age of four, it is very important to read to your children before they can read themselves. Telling stories is as old as time. Almost every type of literature finds its origin with minstrels and troubadours. Telling a story does not generally require any special skills. All you need is someone who is willing to listen. Children make an ideal audience. When they are young, they are very curious and inquisitive. Hence, it does not take that much effort to draw their attention. Telling stories and reading to children is of vital importance. For one thing, the child learns how to listen and it also improves its ability to concentrate, because they will have to pay close attention to what the storyteller says in order for them to follow the plot. Furthermore, their imagination will be stimulated when at certain points during the story they feel they can come up with a continuation, or solutions that lead to a happy ending. Likewise, they become familiar with words they have not heard before (vocabulary), but whose meaning they will leam from the context. And so it would appear that you cannot start early enough. However, it is important to read the right book at the right age. Toddlers, for instance, need a simple story with simple, clearly illustrated pictures. Dick Bruna's Nyntje would be ideal for them. Four-year-olds are ready for a little more adventure. Jip en Janneke by Annie MG Schmidt is a good example of an adventure book that still offers a certain amount of safety in the child's slowly expanding world. It will be clear thar reading to your children is a safe way of introducing them to different worlds and emotions .

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?
Paragraph: Secondly, many crimes are committed when the offenders do not think rationally; therefore, unlikely to consider consequences of their crime. Thus, keeping these individuals in a restricted environment not only would not rehabilitate them, but also let them lose their control again because of torturing acts which happen in prisons. Furthermore, people go to prisons because the society wants to punish them. As a result the society itself might be considered as a violator of human rights and irrational. Government and public could run some communities to teach these prisoners rather than to punish them like animals in cages. Opponents may argue that if prisoners could not modify their behavior or have some mental problems, it is better to keep them away from innocent people in the society. In spite of being right about the responsibility for modifying behaviors, they are wrong about considering prisoners as mentally ill persons. They are not mad mans, they are humans who have committed crime because of losing control over some situations; therefore prisoners should not be infringed from basic rules of human rights such as training courses among other members of society and do not being restricted to prison. Also, punishment is defined as a penalty that is imposed on an individual for doing something wrong. The term rehabilitation is defined as a way to help somebody to return to good health or a normal life by providing training or therapy which is not possible in prisons.

Title: Cigarette advertising should not be banned

Title: Extraterrestrial life: how expensive do we want it to be?

The money would be better spent on causes such as poverty and environmental care. And finding evidence for extraterrestrial life will turn our entire world view upside down, whether this view is religious or not. Title: In the 21st century educational
Paragraph: It may be true that privatization increases efficiency because of increased competition, but the question is at what cost would this efficiency be achieved? If educational institutions were to be privatized and allowed to ' opt-out ' the national curriculum, they would certainly increase their freedom of choice. They would be able to compile their own curricula and add unusual or highly specialized topics to it. The pupils would be able to choose the school that addresses their interests and talents best. This might seem solely as an improvement to the present system where every pupil has to follow a national curriculum, which is too general to be efficient. But if schools try to attract pupils by unusual curricula that consist of popular but simple subjects, there is a danger that the level of general knowledge will dvindle. If the level of nationalization increases a "core-curriculum" should be established. The ' core-curriculum ' should consist of subjects which pupils otherwise might easily ' opt-out', such as mathematics, physics and other difficult subjects .

Title: Marx once said that religion was the opium of the masses. If he was alive at the end of the 20th century, he would replace religion with television

Title: Is the Lack of Empathy in Children a Taboo or a Reality?
Paragraph: Primarily, in order to analyse the role of evil in children, one must try to give a good definition for it. Evil has been explained by all of the religions and philosophical views. However in the modern era, it needs to be defined in accordance with the contemporary doctrines. For instance, one of the founders of the modern philosophy Immanuel Kant defines evil as: craving for something forbidden even though one knows it is not right <R> . Moreover, Kant believes that evil exists in all human beings and is affiliated with free choice <R> . On the other hand, Cambridge professor Baron-Cohen, interrelates the terms evil and lack of empathy and creates his own system of "six levels of empathy" in which zero empathy is also divided to positive and negative. Zero-positive people are conditioned with autism or Asperger's syndrome and although they do not possess empathy they can follow the rules of society. Contrarily, zero-negatives are people with borderline personality disorder, antisocial personality disorder and narcissistic personality disorder. These people are capable of harming other people and do not feel guilt after that <R> . Another approach for the definition of evil comes from the Psychology professor Ana Frichand, who considers the notion of evil nearly impossible to be defined as its position in the sphere of psychology is unsteady and extremely complex, and no definition could thoroughly encompass its meaning for humans <R> . In my mind, it is high time to replace the old-fashioned term evil with a more reasonable and thorough identification bearing in mind that the more we know about it, the better we can deal with it and correctly treat it .

Title: Sculpture as gesture
Paragraph: All of Twombly's sculptures are white. Art historians keep repeating Twombly's dictum that white paint, to him, is marble. However it seems that the whiteness constructs a sphere of the unreal. It is reminiscent of a white sheet of paper which has bits of letters and names written on it, but lacks the coherency of a text. Interpreting the sculptures (tracing their specific depth), we get lost. One of the sculptures might be Pan's pipes; and we may think of the myth of Pan and Syrinx in the first book of Ovid's Metamorphoses. But then, the object might be something else. It keeps changing under the spectator's gaze, like Syrinx changes into the pipes precisely at the moment when Pan believes he has caught her .

Title: Public speaking is done in the public tongue, the national or tribal language; and the language of our tribe is the men's language. Of course, women can learn it. We're not dumb. If you can tell Margaret Thatcher from Ronald Reagan, or Indira Gandhi from General Somoza, by anything they say, tell me now. This is a man's world, so it talks a man's language. (Ursula K. Le Guin, American feminist writer).
Paragraph: Have you ever thought that women usually fail in their carreer or are not able to climb their career ladders higher than men due to their insufficient mastery of language? Of course, the word "language" used in this sense underlies more than simple linguistic profficiency. What is important here is the ability to use our organs of speech to fight our opponents, to firmly state our opinion and make our audience believe it and agree with it. In other words, it is the skill of using language to <?> one's influence on other people, since it is a well-known fact that those who speak and who are heard possess power, whereas shy and silent people usually become subservient to those in power. From the beginning of life on the Earth there existed remarkable distinction between man and woman. This distinction was not only physical and psychological, but their social roles differed as well. Women have always been on a lower social scale than men and were controlled by them. Thus, they were executors of men's orders rather than those who gave them. With the feminist movements the reality began changing and women gained many advantages and to a certain degree learnt the man's language, i.e. language used to exercise power. However, it is not easy to get rid of the influence of the past, its values, norms and traditions. As a result, men continue to be better at public speaking than women are and outpace them in carreer achievements .

Title: Fighting what has been described as a 'rising tide of violence' has recently become a major political and social issue in  Poland. What are the causes in this increase of violence?
Paragraph: Television is often to blame for the increase of violence in Poland. First of all, it has been proved, that television exerts a very significant influence on people's attitudes and opinions. If, then, the opinions presented and favored on television are offensive, rude and instigating, people's opinions and attitudes become the same. That this is really true is conspicuous in attitudes that many Poles have towards clergy and people connected with the Catholic church. Priests and nuns, despised by the majority, often become victims of various acts of violence like robberies, assaults or even rapes. This rule is also applicable as far as Poles' attitudes to other public people and celebrities like politicians, lawyers, actors etc. are concerned. What television says about them is visibly reflected in people's opinions and attitudes. Another significant cause is related to the fact that films presented on television show a great amount of violence. This, first of all, make people indifferent to it. Brutal scenes are now not as shocking as they used to be. They have simply become a natural and normal part of people's lives. Furthermore, as proved in numerous sociological experiments, violent programmes make people violent. The tension, that such broadcasts evoke, is cumulated and sooner or later, has to be released. The rising rate of violence is, then, explainable .

Title: A Cyclist's Lament

Title: Should universities be allowed to solve their financial problems by themselves ?

Despite appreciable and profound changes which took place in all fields of social life, it failed to keep pace with them. According to the reliable evidence, from 1989 on, the dominant policy of the government toward education was to increase the number of available university places for those evincing interest in higher education. This resulted in constantly growing number of students enrolled in colleges and universities throughout Poland. Nowadays, the findings report, there are twice as many university students as there were in 1989. Simultaneously, within the compass of four years, the government has reduced still by a half meagre funds reserved for purpose of education. In consequence of the instanced disposal most Polish universities are confronted with serious financial problems calling for reasonable solutions .

This would not only improve our defence capability but also increase the general willingness to go to the army. Conscripts should have higher daily allowances and better social security. Title: Europe: on the road to abolition of death penalty
Paragraph: From the facts discussed above, it is clear that for the majority of Member States the total abolition of capital punishment was achieved in two stages of which the second was, in general, a lengthy process, it took many years to put in practice. Besides, it has to be stressed that, although countries such as the United Kingdom, Spain, Luxembourg, France, Ireland, Greece and Belgium maintained the death penalty in their laws into the second half of the twentieth century, executions in fact took place quite rarely or else this form of punishment simply remained unused. Actually, a long period of time generally passed between the carrying out of the last execution and abolition of the death penalty. This accepted practice all over Europe leads to the conclusion that when European countries formally abandoned capital punishment they were already abolitionist by fact or even tradition, capital punishment having clearly fallen into disuse in judicial practice for a long time .

Then, the person moves to his/her new family's place and takes part in various rituals, lectures, and discussions led by a guru who often is an experienced psychologist. Title: The Twentieth Century - How will it be remembered?

But do we ever think of the poor farmer of the "Third Estate" who had to give his last potato for the King's evening meal and see his children starve to death? Hardly anybody knows that there were no toilets in the chateau of Versailles and that bathrooms hadn't been invented in 17th century France; neither was underwear, by the way. Although, historically speaking, we know everything about Molière and Corneille, Racine, Colbert, Mazarin or people like Thomas Beckett, Shakespeare, Saint Joan, Jesus Christ, Homer or Julius Caesar, we know comparatively little about how they actually lived .

Title: Is Face-to-Face Learning Better than Online Learning?
Paragraph: Most of the online courses developers refer to online classes as being more convenient, and this is the case certainly since the conformity of bed and food storages at home are unquestionably better option than the rush hour and junk food as a constitutional part of face-to-face classes. Furthermore, online classes are more convenient for the people who have other occupations despite attending classes, and therefore their decision for enrollment at online classes must be encompassed by devotion and self-discipline that exclusively depend on learner's will. However, when it comes to students' attention, and teacher's feedback, these two types of learning unequivocally differ. The teacher in face-to-face classes through various gestures, mimics and facial expressions can maintain the students' attention, whereas the Facebook chat or Instagram extravagant nail polish may easily defocus the students from the online classes, since Facebook is indisputably funnier option than the "very appealing" quantum mechanical components of Nanoelectronics for example. Feedback is one of the crucial components of learning, and students who take part in online classes sometimes may find difficulties in understanding the lecture and even wait to receive feedback for a long period of time because the students/professor communication is developed through texting and joined video calls, whereas completely the opposite in this regard, face-to-face classes facilitate learning since the teacher's feedback is immediate and the seemingly fussy aspects of a lecture are clarified on the spot .

Title: In the 19th century, Victor Hugo said: "How sad it is to think that nature is calling out but humanity refuses to pay heed." Do you think it is still true nowadays?
Paragraph: With the evolution of man came what can be called in the loosest of terms "progress", followed later by industrialization and urbanization. Humans learnt how to harness the creatures and elements of nature so as to facilitate and organize their life. After a series of discoveries and inventions humans succeeded in progressing tremendously along those lines, while at the same time distancing themselves from all other living things, until they eventually forgot their initial intrinsic relation. Gradually, man began to intervene in nature in negative ways and the initial balance was somewhat shaken. However this might have been deemed a small price to pay for what was labeled civilization. The intervention started out as mild but soon became harsher, the implications became visible and by the 19th century Victor Hugo commented accordingly on nature's cry for help and humanity's deaf ear. The sadness of it all, of course, was boned on the fact that all along it was the latter who had been damaging the former. Hugo's utterance in nowadays truer than ever. So much so, that it seems almost premature to have been uttered so long ago, at a time that is considered today as more or less innocent and even rustic .

Title: Women in the Koran.
Paragraph: The Koran is the holy book of the Muslims, containing the writings of Muhammad. It consists of a hundred and forty chapters or soeras of about the same length. In its entirety the Koran comprises 6200 verses. The texts of the Koran are based on Muhammad's sermons and all in all his preaching only lasted for twenty years and is collected in one single book. Muhammad started his preaching between 610 and 612. After long periods of prayers in solitude, he was convinced that he was favoured with apparitions, dreams and revelations. In other words, he had the feeling that some mysterious presence was bringing him a special message from God. Muhammad gradually collected a large group of believers around him. These believers lived according to Muhammad's directions and received his preaching. Over a period of twenty years new pieces were added to the original texts. When Muhammad died in 632 the greater part of central-Arabia, including Mekka, was converted to this new religion, the Islam. With Muhammad's death the era of revelations was concluded. And since he was the last of a long series of prophets, Muhammad was considered the 'seal of the prophets'. From this moment on the Muslims had the Koran, which means ' preaching', at their disposal. At first, Muhammad's sermons were only remembered and passed on verbally, but only less than two centuries after Muhammed's death everything was written down. The Koran is a book full of stories about religious personalities and instructions of how to live one's life .

Title: Those who envision a future world speaking only one tongue, whether English, German, Russian or any other, hold a misguided ideal and would do the evolution of the human mind the greatest disservice. Title: The pernicious influence of fairy tales.

Instead of fighting back, instead of refusing to let herself be humiliated, instead of walking away, Cinderelia stays with the abusive family, keeps doing what they tell her to do, and lo and behold, she is saved by a fairy and a prince. This message is totally wrong: children should learn to stand up for themselves. Everybody should know that there is no such thing as a prince that will save you from your trouble. Anything that you want to achieve in this world you will have to do for yourself. And if you are abused by anybody, you should open your mouth to the right authorities, instead of sitting down and waiting for a fairy, In Hansl and Gretl children will get the message that parents do not love their children unconditionally, and that they may be left when the parents are financially challenged. Unfortunately, things like this do happen, even today, but realistic as these things may be, they are hardly material for a bedtime story. Kids should not be taught to feel insecure about their parents' permanent devotion. Furthermore, this story contains blunt cannibalism and murder. It is in fact this fairy tale that renders children scared of elderly, mean-looking women, whereas statistically most child-abusers (because the message that this tale is supposed to give is: do not take candy from strangers, for you do not know what they are up to) are young, male and have regular appearances .

Title: The children of today - are they to be pitied or envied?
Paragraph: Christmas is a most horrifying season. At no time of the year there are more families split up by dispute, more separated couples and more abandoned pets than around Christmas; there can be no doubt about that, and it is a brave clergyman who still dares to announce the season of happiness, of freedom and peace in his sermon on boxing day. All I remember of last year's Christmas is that my nephew Thomas encountered obvious obstacles on behalf of his benefactors when trying out his present: the present was a game-boy and the obstacle was a slap in the face. Let me briefly report on that incident, which, if you ask me, is worth the while, because it was one of those typical situations of the loving family-type. It all started - not with a kiss, as you might assume, but with the above-mentioned Christmas present. Thomas disappeared behind the Christmas tree with his treasure and wasn't seen again, while the rest of the family desperately kept on singing carols - as if that could have lured him out from his hiding-place! However, the funny thing about last year's Christmas Eve was that although the kid couldn't be seen any more, he was permanently heard of, because the shrill beep-beep of his newly acquired toy kept interrupting the carols. This is how an innocent child experienced a rather ungentle touch of his mother's normally tender palm on his cheek for the very first time, believe it or not. But wasn't it aunt Doris' fault? It was her who had given him the bl... game-boy. Mind you, Thomas pulled through and soon learnt to accept that slaps in the face are a part of life's rich tapestry. That evening, however, I remember Granddad leaning back in his red velvet armchair and giving a melancholic sigh . "When I was little, we didn't have these problems; and we had a far better childhood, I can give you that," he said and then he took another sip of his hot wine and shut his eye-lids .

Title: Equality of Opportunity

There are still discussions about whether women, doing exactly the same job as a man, should be payed the same amount of money as men in identical positions. To me, there is not one good reason why they shouldn't. Women go through the same education as men, they get the same debts as men while studying and they have the same conditions as men have at work .

Title: Some people say that in our modern world, dominated by science and technology and industrialisation, there is no longer a place for dreaming and imagination. What is your opinion?

With the accompaniment of soundtrack music and special effects, we experience the plotline which was lain out for us in a simple way - we get the whole nine. Title: A defence of capital punishment
Paragraph: In what are known as the bad and cruel old days of the dark and dismal Middle Ages, we are led to believe that a bloodthirsty hangman with a black hood on his head and a huge axe in his hand was doing his job in almost every country of the known world. Not only did he execute, however, criminals or murderers. The victims slaughtered at the stake or on the scaffold came from all walks of life: from beggar to best man, from lawyer to lecturer, from queen to dairymaid, from king to knight, from witch to whore. It would have been very easy to sentence somebody to death without a real reason. It were mostly churchmen and feudal lords who abused capital punishment as an instrument of political power in order to reach their ambitious aims .

Title: Women are Discriminated at Work

The caring, cleaning, catering professions are underpaid partly because they are seen as less skilled than "male" jobs. The law discriminates against part-timers: they have to work for more than twice as long as full timers to earn protection from unfair dismissal and have far fewer employment rights. Since the majority of part-timers are women the law is just sanctioning discrimination. Besides only 3% of university professors, 8% of business managers and 15% of medical consultants are women .

Title: Some people say that in our modern world, dominated by science and technology and industrialisation, there is no longer a place for dreaming and imagination. What is your opinion?

Dreams could be seen as a product of imagination; their primary purpose is to provide a chance to escape from reality to a far more pleasant state of mind. Although there are no special devices required in dreaming, imagination can be inspired by various methods, examples of these being music, literature, painting, motion pictures and other forms of art. They give us ideas and images that we can develop further in our minds and apply them to create our own dreams. Technology has an important role in this process, because it can help to transport the ideas from their originators to us. Modern picture and sound reproduction systems make arts available in every home, leaving more time and energy for the art itself to be enjoyed. Therefore, it is justified to claim that technology does not create a threat to dreaming and imagination; instead it can add new dimensions to them .

Title: Conflicts are necessary for healthy relationships.
Paragraph: And when it comes to other types of relationships, like relationships between friends or relationships between family members, I have the same opinion. For example, if you have an argument with one of your family members or friends about some advice they gave you, you should not be mad and think that all they want to do is control your life and intrude your privacy. Because all they want to do is help you to get your problems solved, and they are doing that because they love you and they are probably worried about you. And when you realize that, and think about that conflict you understand that it was really irrelevant and it only showed you how much your family cares about you and wants you to be happy. These kind of conflicts are healthy because they show you how caring and loving your family or friends are, and that can definitely make your relationship with them better. On the other hand, there are those conflicts full of shouting which make you feel really sad and nervous, no matter if it is or is not your fault, and you can't learn anything from them. Those conflicts can only destroy your relationships with the people you love, and you should try to avoid them by talking about problems on time .

Title: Some people say that in our modern world, dominated by science and technology and industrialisation, there is no longer a place for dreaming and imagination. What is your opinion?

But then came technology, because of machines the work became much lighter, people could go on holiday once in a while and had plenty of time to fantasize and dream. In this respect technology has been a blessing for mankind. However this ' blessing ' has now taken on such proportions that for some people it becomes almost impossible to think and dream freely. Technology can be used to stimulate people, to make them form their own opinions, but all too often it is used to impose the opinions and convictions of the people who are in control, on people who are using the technology. From this viewpoint our world is beginning to resemble the worl that Orwell described in "1984", a society that is controlled by a few people who have all the means to manipulate other people in such a way that creativity and fantasy become impossible. If however our technology and media are used in a responsible, "well thoughout" manner, they will stimulate people to think, give them time to do something else than make money, so that free imagination and dreaming become possible .

Title: Violence

Title: Some people say that in our modern world, dominated by science and technology and industrialisation, there is no longer a place for dreaming and imagination. What is your opinion?
Paragraph: The art becomes more abstract in the XX century. It no longer reflects real things or situations - only pure feelings, and it seems to me, that this tendency strikes some kind of a balance between the real world dominated by science and technology and the inner world of a man. Now let's turn to some examples. If we look at literature, what we'll see? In the XX century there appears the phenomenon of "the flow of consciousness" (first it appeared in the novels by James Joyce) - the description of pure feelings, which haven't been thought about consciously, haven't been brought to a strict system. Another example - the story "Morphine" by the famous Russian writer M. Bulgakov. It was written in the form of a diary. This form, of course, had been used before, in the XVIII and XIX century writings, but what is peculiar here is that the style of it is very much alike "the flow of consciousness". It seems to us, that we are reading a real diary of a seriously ill man: thoughts and feelings, which change completely nearly in every sentence, incoherent speech... There are no concrete conclusions or some well formulated thoughts in this story, it is based on pure feelings and emotions. But when we are reading it, we see, how the drug is gradually destroing the personality of the main character. And we finally feel (not understand mentally, but feel) the main idea of this story .

Title: However charged language is abused and whatever misunderstandings it may cause, we still have to live with it—and even by it. Paragraph: Moreover, it is almost impossible to use totally uncharged language, only relatively uncharged discourse can be created. Even though, if the producer of discourse tries to avoid evaluative words and to use neutral ones, the way he uses them will show his personal opinion towards the issue. All words have some initial connotation which is inferred from the meaning of the word. For example, if we took three words describing the quality of an object - good, normal and bad, it would be obvious that "good" carries positive connotation and "bad" carries negative connotation and that both words are charged words. However, if we took a neutral word "normal" it would also provoke some implication, that is not good or not bad. And for instance, if a teacher was asked to evaluate the work of a student and told that it is normal, then such epithet would not be neutral any longer and would carry a negative connotation - the work is not good enough, just normal .

Title: However charged language is abused and whatever misunderstandings it may cause, we still have to live with it—and even by it. Paragraph: The further step from pointing out the relativity of the factual languages authority is establishing the relevance of feelings in human ways of understanding. Ever since the First World War had ended, the positivist Enlightenment's tendencies in the Western world's epistemology were irrevocably lost. The scientific research's concentration shifted toward the chaotic, the unconscious and the subconscious - the forces a human being could barely, if at all, control. The consequence of this, from the linguistic point of view was the invention of an appropriate form to mediate this sort of reality: the stream of consciousness technique. Feelings are far less mysterious than our subconscious mind and are one of the senses through which humans perceive the world. Therefore, it is not in need of separation from the factual understanding. And, whilst the stream of consciousness is widely used as a literary technique which is not applicable to science due to its lack of coherence, emotionally charged language does not interrupt the flow of ideas and present as essential element of human epistemology .

The lake of Aral, which used to be one of the world's largest reservoirs of fresh water, is today more than halfway dried out and surrounded by enormous salt deserts. Athletes not just from the NFL but from other sports as well turn to PED's (Performance Enhancing Drugs) in order to repel severe physical contact and injuries which are in the nature of their respective sports. When it comes to American Football, it should be noted that it is a sport which requires athletes to be at a peak physical condition and to be ready to perform at a high level. In my estimation, athletes should not be allowed to use illegal substances. Instead the NFL should try to limit injuries by banning helmet to helmet hits and hits after the play is over. Thus, the issue of PED usage, the penalties for using them and the attitude of the NFL towards this matter will be further elaborated on in this essay .

Title: In his novel 'Animal Farm' George Orwell wrote 'All men are equal but some are more equal than others'. How true is this today?
Paragraph: And there is more to come! When you go for a walk, just look at people's faces. Just look at them! What do you see? You see a young man's face with a bright smile. Then, it is a girl of perhaps fifteen years old who attracts your attention: she is daydreaming. An old man, sitting on a bench, is talking to himself, or rather, he seems to be talking to his hands but when you come along, you hear him whispering: <*> and nobody stops, not even you. You also see a beggar, just holding out his hands but who does not dare to look at you. You see couples, young and old, loving each other or not, dressed with suits or with faded jeans. All the people that you will see are different, they have different social backgrounds, different family's histories, different friends and even different ways of living. Some are rich, others are poor. Some work fourteen hours a day, others are unemployed. Some are white, others are black. Some dwell in beautiful houses surrounded by marvellous gardens where children can play hide-and-seek, others live in teeny flats in dark areas where children can only play in the streets. Do you still think that we are equal?

Title: Those who envision a future world speaking only one tongue, whether English, German, Russian or any other, hold a misguided ideal and would do the evolution of the human mind the greatest disservice. Western culture has made, through language, a provisional analysis of reality and, without correctives, hold resolutely to that analysis as final. The only correctives lie in all those other tongues, which by aeons of independent evolution have arrived at different, but equally logical, provisional analyses. (B. L. Whorf)
Paragraph: Moreover, each and every reality requires its own means of expression. This point could be well illustrated by an example from the life of Indian peoples. Some Native American tribes perceive reality in a way which is considerably different from the way we grasp it. For instance, they do not have the perception of either time or space. Naturally, their tongues lack the vocabulary for it. However, to compensate for the lack of these their languages possess other concepts which would perhaps seem alien to us .

Title: All armies should consist entirely of professional soldiers: there is no value in a system of military service
Paragraph: Nowadays any modern well-equipped army needs experts to all intents and purposes. For example: as a good surgeon or a good economist as well as a good soldier cannot be educated in one or two years. This is, I think, the most visible problem of the military service in our country and in all eastern countries. It is not possible today to train a specialist in one year. And on the top of it our "educated" soldier will forget nearly all knowledge from the military service in some time anyway. I can see no value in the military service - even I am a woman. Everybody wants to graduate at various colleges in order to get a good job and earn money. But when he finishes the school he has to start his military service, instead of applying the acquired knowledge in the practice immediately. What a nonsense ! That is why many young men feel rather frustrated when they get the call-up papers. They start to think about how to avoid this uselessness... Also the young men who have just started their own business get into the similar situation. What a wasting of the knowledge, time and money too ! You will not fill today young men with an enthusiasm when speaking about what honour it is to defend their native country. These times are past for ever... When you force somebody to something that he does not want to do he starts to offer resistance - consciously or unconsciously. Results certainly cannot be satisfactory then. Title: Should video games be violent or not?
Paragraph: Ever since video games hit the shelves in the stores, moving from the arcade, children could not wait to get their hands on them. These parents couldn't bare the fact that their children might be intoxicated by the graphic violent content in the games and that they might try some of the violent actions in real life as well. Many people outraged by this started demonstrating against violent video games, which resulted in bans and censorship around the world. Unfortunately, these people had nothing to worry about since violent video games are just a harmless way of entertainment and to prove my statement, I will explore the issue from two aspects: from the perspective of a psychologist who only sees video games as either a good or bad behavioral influence and from the perspective of the gamer who sees video games as a form of art and entertainment .

Title: Why do people use (illegal) drugs ?

People say they use drugs because they want to. The important question is why they want to. They do drugs because they want to forget about the reality; not interesting, not exciting enough. Moreover, they say they use drugs because they need to. They need to create illusions about themselves because they are not able to cope with who they are. People say they use drugs because they have to. They have to because they became addicted. They lost this feeling of freedom they had at the beginning. They lost the illusion of freedom they had later. Now they know they are slaves of drugs .

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?
Paragraph: The prisons, as known today, became introduced in 19th century, in the medieval England. According to The Poor Law Of 1601 in England, the poor's responsibility was assigned to parishes (local civil government). These parishes built workhouses to employ the poor on a profitable basis. But this effort came to a failure and The Poor Law Amendment of 1834, standardized the system of poor relief throughout Britain. This law stated that no relief could be given to the able-bodied in their own homes and that all who wanted to receive aid have to live in the workhouses. The conditions in these workhouses were harsh and degrading until the 19th century and by the first half of the 20th century social-welfare services and the social-security systems replaced workhouses altogether. As centuries went on, legal system of many countries advanced. Nowadays the government doesn't kill you for stealing an apple, however, at one time they probably would have. It just goes to show that times change.

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?
Paragraph: Many believe that Rehabilitation is the best way of treating criminals. Rehabilitation (here) means to help someone to live a healthy, useful or active life again after they have been in prison. The assumption of rehabilitation is that people are not permanently criminal and that it is possible to restore a criminal to a useful life, to a life in which they contribute to themselves and to society. One goal of rehabilitation is to prevent habitual offending. Rather than punishing the harm out of a criminal, rehabilitation would seek, by means of education or therapy, to bring a criminal into a more peaceful state of mind, or into an attitude which would be helpful to society .

Luckily, the happy world of commercial culture is with most people in the wealthy capitalist countries every single day. Mass media is an extremely powerful preacher of this religion; heaven can be reached by anyone with the necessary - money. No more prayers! Fashion, videogames, celebrities, new design, Hollywood, Disney, talk-shows, cd-players and holiday resorts make up for all the needs of people. An illustration of this situation is the way in which public figures become a part of the life of the unhappy. Ordinary men and women identify with the "superhumans" on the tv every night and that way forget their loneliness, the lack of emotional life and the whole meaningless of life. They experience adventures, success, love and danger through the characters in tv-series. Rock-industry, for its part, fills the need of the young to identify with a culture which is different from the way of life of adults. Music, videos, stars and magazines become the meaning of life, since there does not seem to be any better purpose. The bliss of buying appeals both to young and old; having certain objects is essential to one's self-respect and social status. All these and the other imaginary forms of commercial culture give consumers so much pleasure that they start to need it. If one gets a dose regularly - a car, a tv-show, a teddy-bear - he continues to experience reasonable happiness and does not have to face reality. So, once again, Marx would argue, capitalist system produces opium to the people .

Of course the days are gone when men fought each other with swords and cudgels while looking sternly into each other's faces. A 20th century bomber pilot never comes face to face with his enemies. All he is asked to do is press a little button, and the infernal machinery of highly sophisticated technology will do the rest. Title: Those who envision a future world speaking only one tongue, whether English, German, Russian or any other, hold a misguided ideal and would do the evolution of the human mind the greatest disservice. Western culture has made, through language, a provisional analysis of reality and, without correctives, hold resolutely to that analysis as final. The only correctives lie in all those other tongues, which by aeons of independent evolution have arrived at different, but equally logical, provisional analyses. (B. L. Whorf)
Paragraph: As mentioned above, a single language has always been the product of such phenomena as militaristic repression or necessity of ecconomic relations (seeking mutual understanding, or obeatience). The coertive nature of a single language imposition (and ideals of any sort ARE imposed) is illustrated by the fact that "natural" languages are always in some way defended - by resistance or by language comissions, i.e. there are always some speakers who don't feel right about losing their mother tongue. There is nothing surprising about that if a language evolves in one sociocultural environment, and then out of a sudden is imposed over another. As well as grammatical system, cultural traditions are matched, continued and developed within the language through time: words for old things are archaic, for novel things they are borrowed or coined (following the demands of the existing language, naturally), deictic references employed for things too self-evident or not too important, unique traditions (e.g. that of politness expressions) are followed. All these things are lost and peoplesuffer where one language is mechanically replaced by another .

Title: Reasons for Joining a Subculture or Cult
Paragraph: Firstly, joining cult or belonging to subculture creates for a young, inexperienced person the possibility to find an easy answer to a question "what is the purpose of life". A person who does not have any established hierarchy of values, and whose life is a source of confusion longs for any kind of order, a set of rules that would enable him or her to lead a happy life. Consequently, popularity of cults among the young comes from the fact that a philosophy propagated by a given cult makes the impression of a ready - made "recipe" for a problem-free and meaningful life. What's more a confused youngster is the first one who will desperately search for an easy remedy or a guide who will set a definite purpose in his or her life. An example that can be useful here, may be Shoko Ashahara's follower - often highly educated people who were attracted to him by his promises of achieving inner peace by means of self-enlightenment. The same phenomenon can be observed in case of young people who decide to join skinheads whose philosophy is known for its radicalism and an overt aggression. The young's interest in skinheads' philosophy may be partly explained by the fact that this particular subculture has clearly defined code of rules in which the division between good and bad is explicit. Thus, if one follows the most radical slogans typical for skins, one can learn and believe that these are the color immigrants and minorities of all kinds that are to blame for confusion and disorder in one's life. In fact, a clearly defined enemy may not bring the meaning to one's life but at least, it is good to know on whom to exert anger. Therefore, it may be said that these are the tempting promises and catchy slogans that appeal most to people who search for a purpose in life, Consequently, words are the most powerful means of persuading people to join cults .

Title: Mass media affect our approach to reality
Paragraph: Mass media and in particular television and computers are among these inventions of the present world which occupy most of our time. They have become indespensable as clothes or cutlery without which we are unable to perform any other actions. From our childhood we sit in front of television and watch all sorts of programmes that teach us about the world. The growing commonness of TV or computer understood as the amount of time spent with them changes the way we perceive the world. More and more we learn who we are, where we are and what is our destination from a film, a programme or information we get from computers. The evidence that mass media affect our approach to reality can be given through analysing children and their response to the phenomenon of television. Children learn how to behave through imitation, what they see they take as a pattern without considering its good and bad nature. As their system of values is not fixed yet, they hardly understand the difference and what is bad and repugnant often becomes good and enjoyable from their perspective. Television is a great source of behaviours ranging from moral to evil ones. Unfortunately the predominant part of what we see in it is socially unacceptable and immoral, moreover it does not reflect real life. On the basis of what we see today in the streets we may draw the conclusion that films do not tell about life but they create life. What is going in the film seems to find its realization in one or the other form in the streets. Directors do not care about the impact of their films upon human psyche, they force into films a lot of murders, violent attacks and cruel behaviours in order to make them more attractive and easier to sell. To support the claim that films or in general mass media affect the reality I can enumerate many examples that would prove its truthfulness. Violence which we often encounter in our daily life is in many cases generated by what was seen or heard in mass media like television or cinema. Especially young delinquents when asked to give their motives say that they only wanted to imitate film heroes or see whether it is such a great fun as it was in a film .

Title: Do people need spiritual beliefs ?

At this point people behave in different ways. The vast majority decide to fully use the charms of earthly life, often rejecting any moral obligations in their contacts with others. This translates into cynical, materialistic behavior of an egoist insensitive to other people. However, for some people the result may be quite the opposite. When one rejects religion not for convenience but on the basis of certain intellectual and phylosophical consideration the lack of need for spiritual beliefs does not have to equal enhanced egoism. On the other hand, in many cases, people realize that material bond of all the living world and consequently the need to live peacefully with others. They often open to other religions, thus gaining a broaden outlook upon the world. This gives rise to conscious altruism - behavior consistent with religious recommendations, however, not determines by church's orders and prohibitions but rather resulting from one's own free will. Thus the effects of rejecting one's beliefs may vary from extreme selfishness to magnified altruism .

Title: Should cannabis be legalized for medical purposes?

It can reduce the pain better than most of the prescribed medicines. Also, it is known that cannabis relieves migraines and the symptoms of chronic diseases <R> . The plant produces a group of chemicals called cannabinoids. The studies of the National Cancer Institute proved that cannabinoids inhibit tumor growth by causing cell death and blocking the development of blood vessels needed by tumor to grow. It was also proven that this group of chemicals stimulates the appetite and can increase food intake <R> . These studies of cannabis show that although it is not a cure, cannabis can provide a natural treatment for diseases .

Title: Should Animal Testing Be Banned
Paragraph: On the one hand, multitudes of people that are either involved in such research processes or are counting on their outcome that determines someone's time to live are widely promoting this method. Primarily, they point out to the contribution to medicine made by such procedures, as the California Biomedical Research Association has announced that nearly all breakthroughs of the past century were made on the basis of animal testing solely. Examples of successful cures are insulin for diabetics, as well as the polio and Hepatitis B vaccines that drastically reduced the number of global occurrences. Secondly, promoters of animal testing claim that animals are the best alternative to a human organism. Namely, it has been found that humans and chimpanzees share 99% of their DNA, whereas mice have 98% genetic similarities to humans. Due to this, researchers encourage the use of animals as opposed to cell cultures in a petri dish in order to get a clear image of the complex correlations between various systems in the organism. Certain researchers state that there are conditions, such as blindness and high blood pressure, which cannot even be subject to tissue testing, while others look for a more sustainable solution and offer computer models that would be made reliable by a large, yet finite number of animal tests. Thirdly, these animal testing supporters point out to the beneficial effects which these testing procedures have on the animals themselves. Examples of animal diseases that have been cured during vaccine tests include tetanus, rabies, feline leukemia and anthrax. Other treatments for animal diseases that developed similarly are pacemakers for heart diseases, as well as cures for hip dysplasia and glaucoma. However, the benefit which managed to gain support from veterinary associations was the one for endangered animal species, many of them on the verge of extinction. Animals that have thus overcome the danger of disappearing include the Brazilian tamarins, the black-footed ferret, Australian koalas and the California condor .

Title: A defence of examinations

I clearly remember the eagerness with which I revised the three times table in my first year at primary school, just to do better in the class test than Stephanie, my best friend. Title: In his novel Animal Farm, George Orwell wrote All men are equal: but some are more equal than others" How true is this today?"

Horses shouldn't be required to do a pig's job, neither the opposite. Our particular necessities should specify our role in the society!" Out of that wicked logic, pigs are able to make a ruling class of themselves. It's a common political maneuver, to separate people because of their differences instead of aggregating for their similarities. By building a society on inequality and privileges, politicians have, to this very day, succeeded in the art of misinformation and misguidance. When we look throughout our political history we see that the similarities between men and pigs are far more convincing than a simple wordplay. For, as Orwell once predicted, it's getting harder to differentiate men from pigs .

Moreover, crops engineered can be designed so perfectly that they can tolerate even the worst weather conditions. We can even use nuclear power as an inexpensive source of electricity. That is to say that only a few grams of uranium can illuminate a city like Thessaloniki for a whole week. Besides science, technology has brought so many changes in order to ameliorate our lives. The invention of computers have brought various benefits to people's life. Years ago, people had to calculate on their own numbers but now everything is calculated with a touch of a button. Also, people had to write and store a large amount of documents on paper but now everything is stored in computer files. In addition, exploring the Internet you can come in contact with anyone no matter how far away he/she is from you; you can also get any information you want. But it is not only computers, or mobile phones that have become part of our life, technology dominates in every field. Even household appliances keep the pace of technology and contribute to an hour limited and easier housekeeping. Also, in the field of industry we can produce so many products in a limited time and without any hard effort .

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?
Paragraph: Deterring criminals from returning back to their old life is the most important purpose of justice systems for putting people in jails. Although many countries consider punishment as the best way of fixing bad behaviors, statistics show that the chance for criminal's recidivism in case of punishment is as high as 64%. Others believe that rehabilitation has a more permanent and lasting effect, because it changes the felon's total system of thoughts and it opens a new window to his life. Another point is that, the rehabilitation programs which are designed for criminals to make them rejoin to society are more appreciated by them for some reasons. First, punishment along with imprisonment has a negative effect on the social value system of offenders so they prefer not to be attacked by society as devil prisoners. Second, harsh punishments imposed on some prisoners have destructive effects on offender's psyche and in some cases these harsh tortures may have counter effects, means that the criminal will come back to society more brutal than the past. Finally, rehabilitation programs seek to define bad behavior as a disease rather than a devil choice of criminal, so rehab helpers who usually work in prisons try to find cures and solutions for disease. This gives rehabilitation a distinct advantage over punishment .

Title: Most university degrees are theoretical and do not prepare students for the real world. They are therefore of little value. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?

Well, that ' thing ' can also be a person looking for a job. The higher the education, the fewer people there will be who have that level of education.
