Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?
Paragraph: The first and foremost duty of the justice system is to guarantee justice and to preclude criminals from harming civilians, not to help them find a job and have a good life. When a person commits a crime they must receive fair judgment and punishment for their actions, they must recognize that what they have done is wrong and punishment is the tool. Imagine a society with a system based entirely on rehabilitation. Someone commits a crime, and instead of going to jail they are sent to a rehab center, learn a trade and receive assistance in obtaining a job. This will ultimately send a negative signal and people will see that criminals are being rewarded. They might thus decide that it is worth it to risk committing a small crime. The upside to this scenario is a low rate of recidivism, yet we have to face legions of new criminals who realize that the benefits outweigh the risks. If we have a justice system concentrating merely on retribution, we will have a much more just society. Punishments act as a deterrence to prevent people from committing or recommitting crimes. You may not have a bunch of happy prisoners being released and going on to be a great benefit to society, but that is not the purpose of the justice system. When someone does something bad they get their just and fair punishment. This fulfills the purpose to satisfy justice and to ensure the safety and well-being of honest law-abiding citizens.

Title: Most university degrees are theoretical and do not prepare students for the real world. They are therefore of little value. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?

The result is that students only study hard for their exams to pass their courses, and learn about facts and theories without knowing their application in real life situation. Generally, what they learn is not of any use for them unless they learn how to use their knowledge. Besides, not all the facts and information can be retained after graduation. Many of us can hardly remember what we acquired from the books we had to read for our exams one year ago. If university teachers and professors make students look at things practically and experience them, whatever they learn will stick to their minds and stay there for a long time. Thus they are not easily forgotten and students can always remember them whenever they required them. For instance, reading a chapter of engineering will not help, but doing experiments will surely improve your engineering skills .

Title: In his novel 'Animal Farm' George Orwell wrote 'All men are equal but some are more equal than others'. How true is this today?
Paragraph: A first example clearly illustrates this: the notion of equality in the communist system. In the nineteenth century, Karl Marx realized that the people under the tsarist regime were kept into subservience by a mighty elite. The adequate system would be the communist regime with no class and no State, i.e. an absolute form of egalitarianism. The establishment of such a system required a class struggle and the appropriation of the means of production by the proletariat. But the communist society such as Marx described is proved to be a Utopia. Those ideas led to the dictatorship of the working class and a new division in classes within the working class itself, with the privileged on the one hand and the less privileged (it's a euphemism) on the other hand. For several years, a lot of Eastern countries have tried to get rid of communism. In the former U.S.S.R., Mikhail Gorbachev tried to move towards democracy without doing away with the communist system. Today, Boris Yeltsin strives towards the same purpose as his predecessor, i.e. a free enterprise economy and a democratic system, but he wants to achieve it by doing away with communism. As a consequence, he is criticized by the other politicians who are reluctant to lose the privileges they had obtained during the golden age of the system. That is why they even try to dismiss him .

Title: Should cannabis be legalized for medical purposes?

Title: What should be done about the ozone hole ?

The ozone layer protects against cancer by absorbing the sun's ultraviolet radiation, which means that its existence determines our existence. Nevertheless, it seems to have been losing its wonderful qualities recently, or, to be more precise, it has been actually disappearing. There is no way to patch the ozone hole in the sky at present, but we can prevent it from getting bigger and bigger .

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?

This confinement withholds prisoners of the endowment of communication with different social strata. Hence, such detachment from a normal society and limiting them to abnormal communities brings about defective socialization. Such defective socialization exerts long-term impacts on psychological and behavioral aspects of prisoners and consequently, chances for correction will be decreased, for they don't have social acceptance and are usually looked down on by others as the inferiors. Title: The Reunification of Germany after four years. A story of success?
Paragraph: When it was time for me to return to Germany, I made up my mind to do as if nothing had happened. There could not be much changements, I thought, for a person living hundreds of miles away from the former frontier. However, with all those asthmatic Trabbies turning suddenly up on our roads, I found it more and more difficult to close my eyes to what was happening. On the Christmas following the official reunification of the two German States I received a card from my Japanese pen friend. She told me she was sure that I would have a blessed Christmas with my country being again reunited into one. However, I felt nothing of that sort. A short time before, my parents had paid a visit of a few days to the city where they had lived as a young couple. They had found their house in ruins, with the windows broken and the roof coming down. Documentaries on TV about the cities they had known and loved and that were now completely run-down had added to their bitterness, and they had vowed that, should they be a hundred years old, they would never again set a foot in that part of Germany .

Title: Is reading a book better than watching its movie adaptation?

Title: Is capital punishment different in kind from other sorts of judicial sanction? If it were shown to be effective in deterring  crimes of certain sorts, would that justify its use?
Paragraph: Juha Räikkä introduces two competing theories for the justification of punishment. According to the retributional theory to punish criminals is justifiable simply because criminals deserve to be punished. The advocates of this theory define the concept of crime as an act that requires a punishment; the question of its justification vanishes into air. This sounds reasonable in brutal cases such as murders and rapes, which are acts that are usually considered to be morally wrong. But does a person who breaks the law in order to save somebody's life deserve a revenge? From the moral point of view it would certainly be worse if he obeyed the law and let somebody die because of that. The theory works if we assume that breaking the law is always morally wrong and that it is morally right to punish criminals; and the latter assumption is the very question where I am trying to find an answer - the retributional theory does not help me very much .

Title: Marx once said that religion was the opium of the masses. If he was alive at the end of the 20th century, he would replace religion with television

The researchers find, however, that more and more people take this symbolic reality for granted. Most viewers lack a critical attitude and certainly this can be very dangerous; presenters may say what they want, people believe them. TV then turns out to be a dangerous medium : it can easily be used for manipulation. This we have been able to see during the Gulf War : from the beginning on all media were occupied with it and TV became the most important means of communication between Bush and Hussein. Afterwards a lot of information turned out to be deliberately wrong; it had just been used to mislead Saddam Hussein. It also proved that coverage had been censured more than ever before. The American authorities had wanted a "clean" war, as a result of which TV showed few pictures of victims and always talked about precise bombing, while only 10 % of the bombing had been precise .

Title: Most University degrees are theoretical and do not prepare us for the real life. Do you agree or disagree?
Paragraph: I believe all my colleagues will agree that we would have much more benefit if most of our teachers were native speakers of the English language. What we need most is to communicate as much as possible in English, because when we graduate and start looking for a job this will be of primary importance. Whichever career we embark upon, we will always need to speak effortlessly and confidently. One possible profession that an English Philology graduate can pursue is that of a school teacher or a university professor. We can hardly imagine an educator who has difficulty in talking in English, can we? Such a teacher will not only find it hard to get a job, but he/she will also be of little value for his/her students. A person with an English Philology degree is also likely to hunt a position in a company dealing with British and/or American firms. He/she will have to negotiate the conditions of a contract, any details about a settlement or will perhaps have to arrange any appointments or schedules. In either case. or rather, in any case, he must be a confident and fluent speaker of the English language. A third possible occupation is that of the translator and/or interpreter. In that event, a person needs to have a feel for the language, something that can be acquired only by having a constant contact with native English speakers. We need to be encouraged to speak more and the way is by having British and/or American teachers. Thus, we learn to think in English, because if we are unable to think of a word in the process of talking, we need to explain it in English. If the teacher is Bulgarian we prefer to say it in Bulgarian, rather than straining our minds to figure out a possible explanation. Perhaps, some of us are not aware how important that is. However, we will have to discuss some important issue with an English speaker almost certainly in the future and if we fail, we will be furious and regret that after studying English for 15 or even 20 years we are still unable to conduct a simple conversation .

Title: Is reading a book better than watching its movie adaptation?

Inside the book there are pages and pages of various descriptions which create this world that we are drawn to starting from the characters, environment, food, clothes to the emotions and body language. When reading a book you create the look of the characters in your mind lead by description, you envision places, you become one with the book as you experience all those emotions and sometimes the movie adaptation may fail to fulfil your needs as a reader. Movie adaptation unfortunately in most cases cannot bring this description to life in the right way and in full measure, you may be completely disappointed by the choice of actors either because of the physical appearance or because you are not satisfied with their acting skills. Furthermore, some parts of the book have to be deleted from the movie script since there is not enough room to include them within specific time frame which can be resulted in wrong scene choices and loss of the integral part of the story. While reading a book everything seems natural like it is supposed to be that way, you accept abilities of a character even if they are supernatural. Sometimes this natural view can be ruined if too much of special effects are used in movie adaptation which puts you back to reality and shatters your vision of the book. Sometimes movie adaptation can be completely different from the book, some characters can be added that may confuse you if you read the book and they do not exist there, some scenes can be added as well which may be a good thing and bad, if these scenes explain some parts that are confusing in the book than they are justified .

Title: Feminists have done more harm to the cause of women than good
Paragraph: Very often things have gone even worse. Indeed, the outcome of the feminist revolution has generally been that, by stressing the linguistic inequalities, the male versus female opposition has been reinforced. To take a simple example, women believed that the term "mistress" had a negative influence on their social image because it had a sexual connotation. The masculine counterpart had, on the other hand, no such connotation. Thus, using "mistress" to refer to a woman was seen as demeaning whereas using "master" to refer to a man stressed his social power. Therefore, women believed that the use of "mistress" would strike a blow at their position in society, and they wanted that word to be crossed out of their vocabulary. By doing this, women only made the linguistic inequality, most people were unaware, of more striking .

Title: Is freedom of speech an illusion?

There are millions of people commenting on news and other people's comments on web sites and social media at this very moment. If asked why they write what they write, one of the most common answers would be a variation of "because I have the right to". We use one of our most precious rights to insult people who think differently, to claim supremacy over others, or simply to mock because sometimes being "smart" of "funny" in our virtual lives makes us feel better about ourselves. We rarely stop to think that a lot of people died throughout history so that we would have an opportunity to speak out and not be punished for it. A lot of people died rebelling against cruelest of regimes, demanding their voices to be heard. Those voices asked for justice, freedom and equality that are given to us by our democratic laws. Title: Is reading a book better than watching its movie adaptation?

Even though the author had his own idea of the way the book would be perceived, the reader does live through it subjectively, in a completely new and subjective way, which couldn't be said about movies. Movie is just one way of perceiving the story of the book, because it is directed by one man. Thus, the audience sees what the director wanted them to see. Readers usually don't like movie adaptations because they omit or substitute things that the director thought unimportant. We could take an example from the Harry Potter books and movies, there are many things changed and omitted but the one that was most disturbing was in the "Harry Potter and deathly hallows". In the very ending of the whole journey through that magic land was, a battle of course, the most anticipated battle from the beginning of the franchise. The way it was played out through the book and through the movie was entirely different, especially when it comes to the scene of Voldemort's dying. As in the book, he was simply supposed to land on the cold floor and just remain the empty shell he had always been, but in the movie, his death was showed more impressively, he was falling to pieces and flying all around. The author intended to emphasize that he was hollow his entire life so that was supposed to be the same way he would die and that is something the movie, however good, could have never given .

Title: English is an easy language to learn
Paragraph: It is usually the case that the first encounter with English, for both the adult and teenage learners, is very rewarding. Everyone who takes up learning is acquainted at least with several phrases or well-known words they have come across in their everyday life, watching satellite programmes, playing computer games or simply from their mother tongue, which is replete with loanwords. The fact that English nouns on the whole are not subject to inflection and verbs do not conjugate makes the beginners feel confident and motivated to learn such a language that appears to be ' much easier than Polish and Russian'. For those who are risk-takers speaking does not pose any problems at all, as they simply make use of the scope of vocabulary they have acquired so far and try to make themselves understood. Once the learners have overcome the fear of mastering the infamous sixteen tenses and the rules governing articles, over which every beginner loses sleep, they come to the conclusion that English grammar is digestible and even comparatively easy. Another argument supporting the alleged effortlessness of mastering English, or at least the ability to communicate easily in it, is the abundance and accessibility of pedagogical material, both authentic and inauthentic, designed for teaching this language. Taking advantage of the comparative studies of English and Polish languages enables teachers to predict and eradicate possible errors made by learners and thus facilitates learning .

Title: The pleasures of cycling
Paragraph: I can still very well remember the last package holiday I went on. We were all sitting in a large coach rolling at a constant speed on noisy and busy motorways towards our destination - Venice. There was hardly any room to move in the coach. Every seat was taken, and as the air-conditioning did not work as it was supposed to, I could hardly breathe in the hot and sticky air inside. I tried to sleep now and then, but either fell over on my neighbour's shoulder and awoke in a shock, apologizing several times for my embarrassing behaviour, or I leaned my head on the window to my left and felt that I had a stiff neck when I opened my eyes again after my little nap. During our trip, we had a break twice just next to the motorway, and it was just long enough to use the toilet in the restaurant next door and to walk ten metres up and down in the midst of the exhaust fumes surrounding us. Needless to say, the whole journey was a torture for all of us sitting in the coach, and everyone was relieved when we finally reached our destination in the late afternoon .

Title: Lying is immoral and should always be condemned
Paragraph: There are many disputable situations in which people of certain professions lie to others, as they claim, for those people own good, for example, patients. It is generally accepted that in the course of patient's treatment, his or her mental attitude plays equal part as medical care. Therefore doctors believe that the patient's willingness to recuperation can work miracles and should not be weakened by acquainting patients with the knowledge of their real state of health. This problem seems to be extremely difficult to judge, however the intentions of doctors lying to their patients cannot be questioned and if only the results are good the permission to do so may be given. The only problem is how to evaluate doctors who have failed in their attempts to cure their patients using such a method. It may be said that if they were given the right to lie, any consequences must be accepted and obviously they cannot be condemned for they have been trying to save human life. Unfortunately, in every day life people resort to lying very often and for more mundane reasons than previously mentioned. They make a frequent use of a lie to win other people's acceptance, approval, to hide their inefficiency. They tend to create they own world in which they play the role of imposters who are unable to cope with the reality. In such situations lying appears to be a sign of cowardice and, however, any generalization blackens the problem, is really a subject to condemnation. One more instant of lying which interesting and therefore worth mentioning is a fact of children not telling the truth to their parents. It may be heard that for such a situation only children are to be blamed. Whereas, to do the justice it should be said that the fault might be found on parent's side who are not always prepared to accept the truth from their children. This is how children are somehow forced to lie to their parents. Obviously this argument cannot be used to justify any act of child's lying, but the word ' condemnation ' should be used very carefully in this case. As it has been tried to prove, not every lie which is immoral deserves condemnation. The fact that some deeds are described as immoral does not mean that they must be condemned. There also situations in which it is impossible to say whether certain activity was despicable. However, lying is generally considered to be immoral, the reason for which it is deplored may change the way it is perceived .

Title: Should criminals be imprisoned or rehabilitated?
Paragraph: Every properly-working society needs to punish and clearly condemn criminal actions, especially severe and brutal ones. By doing so, we convey the acknowledgement that victims have been wronged and we achieve justice. In reality, it takes months, even years until criminals are being apprehended, taken to court and sentenced. In order to work, punishment must be applied to the maximum intensity. Furthermore, criminals must be sentenced immediately. Otherwise, the effects are temporary and we provide opportunities for other offences to be reinforced. Also, it is important that low-risk offenders be separated from high-risk offenders. If not, long-term contact between the two groups would only increase the risk of recidivism. In my opinion, long prison sentences should be appointed to brutal criminal deeds, such as heavy robberies, armed robberies, human trafficking, drug dealing and smuggling, organized crimes, genocides, murders or multiple murders performed by the same criminals. More so, the committers of these crimes need to be put in a solitary confinement and sentenced with the highest possible punishment. For example, if the average life expectancy is seventy-five years of age and the offender is thirty years old, than the sentence should be forty-five to fifty years long. This way, reoffending more serious crimes in the future will be prevented.

Title: Those who envision a future world speaking only one tongue, whether English, German, Russian or any other, hold a misguided ideal and would do the evolution of the human mind the greatest disservice. Western culture has made, through language, a provisional analysis of reality and, without correctives, hold resolutely to that analysis as final. The only correctives lie in all those other tongues, which by aeons of independent evolution have arrived at different, but equally logical, provisional analyses. (B. L. Whorf)
Paragraph: Secondly, another negative consequence is concerned with ethical and moral issues. Title: The Olympic Games are an expensive waste of time.
Paragraph: That, of course, is great for the town and its image. And we at home? We had the excitement and suspense of two weeks of a highly interesting TV programme. We had heroes to identify with. To suffer with. To celebrate with. How dull our lives will be now for many months until the Summer Games will start. Where are they again? Hopefully, we will have a great Olympic summer. It's the people who want the Games. That's what already the ancient Romans said. But wasn't there still something else in this well-known Latin saying? Circenses - games and - panem bread! We who were watching the Games certainly have bread and more than that: Also enough to spread on our bread; and our beloved stars at Albertville or at any other Olympic town in the past and - hopefully - in the future have enough to eat as well and enough champagne to celebrate their success or to swallow down their bad luck, but what about all those in the slums of our big cities or out on the streets who have to live on social security? Their stomachs are not filled by three medals for Germany. If they happen to have a TV set of their own they might have feelings very different from those the well-off people have while watching the Olympic competitions. Don't get me wrong: I, too, enjoy watching "Olympia" sometimes. It's not that I am completely against the Games, but there is a certain uneasy feeling if I think about the costs and about the sense of it all. Because I can't make more sense of Olympic Games than of the fireworks at New Years Celebrations. They are both splendid to look at, but after a very short time, everything is forgotten and nothing remains but a lot of rubbish. And a large amount of money is wasted .

Title: Face-to-face communication is better than other types of communication, such as letters, email or telephone calls.
Paragraph: First of all, technology-based communication can reduce possible mistakes made in communication. Technology-based communication may lack in creating a stronger bond or intimacy between people because it does not provide both verbal and nonverbal factors at the same time as a face-to-face communication does. Conveyance of nonverbal factors has great impact on communication than verbal factors. However, this makes technology-based communication a much more effective method in certain occasions. Sometimes, it is better to hide your feelings because it can create worse outcomes. Nonverbal factors, including facial expressions, tone, and appearance are hard to hide and they might greatly affect the meeting negatively. On the other hand, technology-based communication can prevent misunderstandings. Since emails are in texts, you can go over what you wrote and revise it as many as you want. Phone calls seal your facial expressions, so you can hide your feelings if you like and avoid possible disputes with your business partners or friends. For instance, important contracts, inquiries, or arrangements are almost always in written form rather than verbally transmitted to avoid any mistake or miscommunication. Likewise, face-to-face communication can be a bad choice in some situations .

Title: Conflicts are necessary for healthy relationships.

Title: Reasons for Joining a Subculture or Cult

There will be always some charismatic leaders who will be able to captivate thousands of people by means of temptetive promises of a good life. Additionally, there will be always some people who will be trying to find a vent for their disagreement with social and economic norms, as well as the way for promoting a new lifestyle. And though, the philosophies of subcultures and cults may change and multiply in a course of time, these informal groups will always exist. This is because they will be providing confused people with a sense of stability and harmony in life as well as with possibility of creating one's identity - or at least with the substitutes of these values .

Title: All art is quite useless

Title: Any method of teaching is better than lecturing, which is boring and totally useless for students
Paragraph: The students need a lot of different activities during the classes. Students should get more responsibilities for their own work. Everything should not be given on a silver salver. The best way of awoiding this would be small tutorial groups, with 5-8 students. The teacher would just be an instructor, but the work would be done by the students. A short instruction from the teacher in the beginning and then the students would take over. However, there is one big problem, money. We are today living in a time when the sate have to save money everywhere. And that affects schools and universities. We are forced to have big groups, because if we had for example ten small tutorial groups instead for one big, the teacher should be given more salary and that is not possible because there is no extra money. This would of course be nice for the teacher (more money) but there could be an other problem too. Because we are saving, there are not enough teachers and if tutorial groups were possible, do they have time? Some teachers may have several different classes and may be teaching in different schools during one day. Maybe this will be possible on day. If the only possibility is to have big groups, there are several good ways to make the lecture successful. Depending on the topic, you can do almost anything. If the topic is for example literature and you are discussing a book, you could show a videofilm on the book (of course if there is one). You could go to see a play at the theathre, or do some team work in the class room. Make the students read aloud poems or put up a play in the class. The possibilities are enormous. Every topic is not as easy as literature, grammar could be quite hard to act. But instead of teaching grammar in the traditional way, = by heart, the students could write an essay and do some research by themself .

Title: Mass media affect our approach to reality
Paragraph: There is no denying to the fact that television plays the most essential role as far as participation in our life is concerned. The process of its influence begins early in childhood as many children begin viewing as infants. Children can and do watch television programmes that tell them about the secrets of adult life and introduce them to themes they normally would encounter much late in life. Young children are exposed to the news almost every day. Most so-called family programmes deal with themes with which children would not have been familiar even 25 years ago. Through mass media we know and develop intimate and sometimes intense relationships with public figures of all kinds. The stars of television ranging from announcers to rock performers to politicians have become pseudo-intimate acquiaintances. Seeing political events, the expression on faces, and the use of hands or eyes during an interview adds some dimensions to political figures and influences the way we perceive them. Therefore, television seems to play the essential role in the development of attitude, emotion, and social behaviour, and intellectual functioning. Since television is filled with dramas about human interactions-about family life, love, sexuality, emotion, and problem solving-viewers use some of these messages and images in their own interactions with others. People, including children, respond emotionally to TV content, but those responses differ, depending on the characteristics of the responder. Polish TV contains a great deal of violence. There is extensive evidence that television violence can influence aggressive attitudes and behaviour. There is little explicit sexuality, but there are many indirect and suggestive references to sexual behaviour. All of this has played an important role, along with other factors, in weakening traditional ties of church, ethnic group or neighbourhood. Our earlier ties to family, church and appropriate patterns of behaviour have eroded .

Title: English is increasingly becoming a world language. Why do you think this is so? What should  be the characteristics of a world language?

Therefore most of the learners of the language study the same form of English. Due to English being spoken in so many countries it should not be a problem to convince people of its importance in international communication. But how could an inventor of an artificial language make people learn it? English is continuously spreading regardless of the opposition it is meeting for instance in France and Germany. They have tried not to use English in advertisements, shop names and traffic signs, but have had little succes. So, today Frenchmen say "jeans" and Germans have more stores with English names they have ever had .

Title: The prison system is outdated. No civilized society should punish its criminals: it should rehabilitate them
Paragraph: In this perspective, the alternative punishment ' arbitration ' was introduced; the criminal will be confronted with his behaviour and the consequences and he has to look for solutions; for instance getting involved in social work. The victim can play an active role in this process of ' arbitration', so that he or she can get fully compensated. After that, the criminal can return to society. This process of arbitration between victim and criminal (before the punishment in prison has been set) has been tested in several courts and surprising results support the experiment. Judges really consider the arbitration between victim and criminal. And many people believe that if the system gets introduced officially and can include convicted criminals, the overcrowding in prisons can be seriously reduced. Quick and efficient rehabilitation is emphasized and less money will go down the drain because it is spent sensibly. The criminals will benefit from this system because they can return to society without being sent to jail to pick up a bad habit or two in the process .

Title: Cellulars should be abandoned

Title: The role of censorship in Western society
Paragraph: Now the first question that arises is who is going to appoint those whose job will be to keep our eyes, ears (noses, hands?) clean. As a general rule, censors are chosen by official representatives of the State. Not only do they designate censors, but they also determine the guidelines according to which these will have to carry out their duty. In other words, it takes power or money - but is this not one and the same thing? - to have the right to define a code of morality with which thinkers, writers, journalists, film directors, musicians, artists in general, must comply. Should they not, they would most likely be compelled to remove or adjust those parts of their work which do not enjoy the censors' approval. After all, we may be tempted to believe that this process may at times have been beneficial to the cultural standards that prevail in our society .

Title: In his novel Animal Farm, George Orwell wrote "All men are equal: but some are more equal than others" How true is this today?
Paragraph: With reference to nations that have transitioned from totalitarian regimes to theoretically democratic ones, the Balkan countries may be used as fitting examples. Although this is the case, in practice, most of these nations are far from experiencing true democracy and equality, especially when it comes to the distribution of political power. Macedonia is currently a typical case of a "democratic" country, where state administration has been usurped by the political party in power, where the political, social and legal system which is supposed to be administering democracy is experiencing a certain malfunction. This may be seen as the common case of the abuse of power by those in control. Presently, Macedonians are experiencing the absurd situation of Animal Farm, where all men are equal and should be equal before the state, but where a certain few are more equal than others. The current President has pardoned every single politician from the biggest political parties who have been under investigation for corruption, and has ceased all legal procedures against them. Ultimately, this is a proof that absolute power, such as that in the hands of totalitarian regimes like North Korea, is more present here than expected .

Title: Most university degrees are theoretical and do not prepare students for the real world.

Title: Is capital punishment different in kind from other sorts of judicial sanction? If it were shown to be effective in deterring  crimes of certain sorts, would that justify its use?
Paragraph: This short discussion of the main points linked to the problem of capital punishment leads to the final question. Is death penalty justifiable? Though, as we have seen, it is a complex issue, but an instinctive answer is simple. Death penalty is not justifiable. First and foremost, it does not meet the requirements of a civilized penal code, because it denies the criminal the right to reform. Second, it does not seem to be an efficient deterrent. Statistics are ambiguous in this respect as it is difficult to account for all sociological factors involved. It also raises numerous moral questions and does not solve any. Finally, it does not cure the society from evil, but only removes responsibility for it from all of us, to one, to the offender. Capital punishment is nothing more than a confession on the part of the community of a failure to protect, educate and support its every member .

Today, with little more than a keybord and some technical knowledge, that same terrorist has the potential to cause immense chaos and threaten thousands of lives. By shutting down the telephone exchanges of a major city, for instance, he is able to stop all the communication to and from and within that city. The disruption would bring all business to a stop causing millions of dollars of loses. The most dangerous, however, is the threat of human lives. Consider the consequences if, for example, a terrorist were gain access to a traffic air control network. A terrorist understands the potential of high-technology equipment as a terrorist weapon. With the aid of computers terrorist have become more efficient, more ruthless and far more lethal .

Title: Natural Equality

Many kinds of discrimination are discussed widely today. We speak of racial or ethnic discrimination, or discrimination against homosexuals. Some claim that women are still discriminated against in many areas of life. Many fear that the possibility of discovering "defects" on an embryo, may lead parents to children with "unwanted" genes to choose abortion. Title: Is reading a book better than watching its movie adaptation?
Paragraph: Each new popular book or book series attracts the attention of people in the movie business, though most often the results may not satisfy the people who followed the story with enthusiasm from page one or make an impact on the newcomers unfamiliar with the book. A book of fiction is a specific medium of entertainment that allows the readers to ease into the story, dwell on the minor details important in pages to come and cherish the lavish descriptions of places, clothes and inner thoughts of the main heroes or villains. On the other hand, a movie adaptation, often aiming only to duplicate the visual imagery, without quite capturing everything else, in most cases lacks the inner magic of the original. The books and the movies based on them are quite different mediums that develop the story in different ways, so the question here is, can a movie be a faithful representation of the book?

Title: Fighting what has been described as a 'rising tide of violence' has recently become a major political and social issue in  Poland. What are the causes in this increase of violence?
Paragraph: Poland's economic situation is not good. There is a considerable problem of unemployment. Many people are out of work. However, those who have stable jobs do not feel secure at all. Wages they get do not usually allow them to satisfy all their needs. Most of the money is spent on high taxes and with the rest it is hard for an average Polish family to make both ends meet. Furthermore, the process of privatization that Poland had recently gone through, resulted in mass lay-offs which gave people the feeling of insecurity. All those problems gave rise to social dissatisfaction and anxiety both on a state and a family scale. Young people growing up in such conditions have to share their parents' worries about money. They can not afford a bicycle or a computer which the better-off families can. They feel rancour and anger. Sometimes they decide to resort to illegal means or to violence to get some money. They may be driven by the will to help their parents but also by their own desires to possess valuable things. Yet, it is not only young people who commit crimes. Also, middle-aged people who could work legally choose illegal ways to get rich quicker. Under those circumstances violence serves as means of releasing anger, anxiety and dissatisfaction .

Title: The benefits of studying a year abroad

By making these cultures and languages accessible to students from other countries cultural diversity will be maintained. Sending students abroad can also serve to diminish prejudice and discrimination. When people get to know inhabitants of countries they had prejudiced ideas about, they will probably realize that their ideas were not based upon facts. This might even help to improve relations between countries in the long run. A further advantage for the European Community is the fact that it needs graduates who have had an international education and who can travel through Europe without experiencing communication problems or cultural differences. For they have been educated to think not only in terms of regional and national purposes, but especially to consider international ones .

Title: Is Face-to-Face Learning Better than Online Learning?

Title: In our time, political speech and writing are largely the defense of the indefensible. (…) Thus political language has to consist largely of euphemism, question-begging and sheer cloudy vagueness. (…) Political language (…) is designed to make lies sound truthful and murder respectable, and to give an appearance of solidity to pure wind.  (George Orwell)
Paragraph: However, the usage of euphemisms has its negative effects. There is a tendency to overuse euphemisms or make advantage of them by hiding the real meaning under the misleading and deceiving phrase. This process is called doublespeak and is used to misguide and affect audience. Sometimes a faulse euphemism is used to confuse audience: e.g. domestic animals, like pigs, cows, chickens etc., can be called "grain-consuming animal units" or an anconscious person can be said to be in "a non-decision-making form". But much more often doublespeak is used to hide some facts or to affect audience's attitude towards certain events or things. For instance, American media avoided calling military actions in Iraque a war, and instead it was refered to as "peace enforcement actions", "program against terrorism" and many other attitude-shaping phrases. Similarly, a plane crash can be called "an uncontrolled contact with the ground". These examples illustrate that in the political discourse doublespeak serves as a tool for propaganda. Doublespeak together with weasel words, such as "new", "improved" and "fast" (e.g. a new parliament usually consists of the same parliament members, an improved budget has only one or two new aspects included but it does not mean it is better then the previous one) affect audience's attitude towards governments, their actions and form certain oppinion which create circumstances and conditions for preveiling political ideals and ideas .

Title: The functions of a university
Paragraph: A relatively new challenge for universities is that of internationalisation. Internationalism is an inherent element in the activities of a future university, because knowledge is universal. Of course one of the principal objectives of universities is to serve their own countries. But the fact is that almost any major problem in today's society has cross-cultural aspects. Especially a small country like Finland depends in many different ways, economically and culturally, on what happens elsewhere in the world. Therefore it is easy to see that no university in search of solutions to modern problems can afford to isolate itself from the world's scientific community. Fortunately, universities seem to be aware of this and are making efforts to create different forms of co-operation on global scale. Student exchange programmes have become increasingly popular during the last couple of years. In my opinion it would be very important also for university teachers to visit other countries, to gain new knowledge and learn to adapt themselves to foreign conditions. Whatever they learn in this way they should of course make use in their teaching and pass on, through their students, to the surrounding society. In many respects the university can thus serve as a bridge between Finland and the surrounding world .

Title: The prison system is outdated. No civilized society should punish its criminals: it should rehabilitate them

But equally obvious is the need for a much wider use of punishment methods. For minor crimes and first time offenders, mild consequences like community service may in many cases be the best solution. To let someone who has defaced public property with spray-paint and graffiti make up for his transgression by washing it away and perform, let's say 50 hours of free work for the local cleansing department, will, in most cases, be a much better deterrent than ordinary imprisonment, and society as a whole will also benefit from this solution .

Title: Money is the root of all evil

Title: Should Animal Testing Be Banned

These living beings are kept in cages for all their lives, they are traumatized by the daily torture and by the different kinds of drugs given; they are left with open wounds, blinded eyes, etc. But, is this really necessary? Is it morally and ethically justified? Every living being, not just people, owns the ability to think, to feel pain, to show affection and aggression, to cry, to love. Therefore, animals should not be excluded from the fact that they too deserve to live in freedom and happiness. Hence, they do not deserve to be blinded for beauty, so this testing should be banned for being immoral, archaic and unreliable .

Title: Is reading a book better than watching its movie adaptation?

Let's take, for example, the Game of Thrones series, based on A Song of Ice and Fire, written by James R. R. Martin. The TV show appeared to be well accepted and good, but it cannot bring the excitement that the book carries with. Many scenes are omitted or incomplete, whereas special attention was paid to some unimportant happenings that have not even been in the book. Everything is done for commercial and audience needs, even though if it completely changes the plot. So, in short lines, book is written by an author and its movie adaptation is shortened and customized version, which often does not have the same artistic value. Nowadays, movies are made to be sold and to bring a certain amount of money. Same cannot be said for books. It is well known that many famous artists died penniless and in misery. They did not have any profit from their writings, but their works remained immortal, as a reminder of cultural heritage. Books can teach us new vocabulary that has not been used in everyday speech; they can reflect problems that were characteristic for an era; they are good for brain training; they make you think about life; they always carry a message .

Title: Violence on television
Paragraph: As it has been previously mentioned, the harm done by violence in television affects the viewer and his mentality. Various tests done by psychologists and sociologists show that every second person in the world watching different programmes that contain violence turn depressive and develop certain fears to what is going around them. They become more suspicious and distrustful of other people. So on the whole, it can be said that violence in this case creates mistrust among people. It especially refers to the informative description of crime. However, the violence on television is extremely dangerous and affective to children and teenagers. This is because their mentality is still developing and therefore is more likely to form an inappropriate view of life. I truly think that the violence on television is equal to the violence observed in the family as long as the TV is not switched off. Clearly, if a child sees it every day, he considers it a normal thing. Besides, children often identify themselves with the characters on television and normally imitate their actions. Some of us remember cases when teenagers, inspired by fighting movies, try to do the same and usually even injure their friends. What is more, I think children have a wrong perception of people committing acts of violence that are shown on television. Usually those people remain in their memory as heroes or speaking in slang, "tough guys". That is why they often try to take the same role and step by step they become aggressive. And finally, violence painfully affects sensitive children. Growing up with that much of violence on television their minds develop fears that can be really harmful to their mental state and future life. It can also affect in more tricky and dangerous way where the psychologists cannot help so easily. Living his life with violence on television a child gathers and hides everything inside and in his later years he would suddenly show his unpredictable aggression. So, it is obvious, that violence on television has enormous negative influence .

Title: In his novel 'Animal Farm' George Orwell wrote 'All men are equal but some are more equal than others'. How true is this today?
Paragraph: A lot of political and religious systems as well as other forms of organizations such as the army or the school system illustrate Orwell's observation. It reflects the widely held opinion that all people are born equal, that is with the same rights but that in the course of their life, as everybody is free to become what they want to, the fittest being those who combine strength and fighting spirit will reach superior positions. Communism aimed at overcoming Darwinism and tried to equal out the difference between the strong and the weak by treating everybody equally. But the well-meant concept didn't work. The communists finally had to acknowledge that the interaction of strong and weak, of superior and inferior is necessary for the survival of the whole group. That is that people need variation and some stimulant so that they can be productive and creative. Nowadays the formerly communist Easteuropean countries tend to adopt the capitalistic and democratic system of Western nations. Both market economy and elections enable those who want to move up the scale to do so. The link between politics and economy is underlined by the fact that the state provides for the socially weak by giving them part of the affluent's welfare, thus also aiming at equal opportunities for everybody, which obviously aren't provided by nature. In economics, the difference between the strong and the weak is defined in terms of the rich and the poor or in terms of the managing director and the simple worker, standing at the opposite ends of the same scale. In politics, the difference between those at the top and those at the bottom is expressed in terms of the leaders in power making the decisions and the masses, the so-called subjects or underlings .

Title: Automation causes more problems than it solves

Title: The prison system is outdated; no civilized society should punish its criminals. It should rehabilitate them. Do you agree or disagree with the statement above?
Paragraph: There are criminals who have committed many crimes against the society and dealt damage to many; but imprisoning them is not the best option here. Hence, finding another way to put them to good use will be more satisfying for both sides. These criminals must be given a chance to repay for the damage they have done; for instance, if one has transgressed the laws of a specific company, say, a network security system, he or she must be given a chance to atone for his or her crimes by helping that company in order to strengthen their security; thus, that company will be able to withstand even stronger attacks in the future. They were not allowed to be individuals and their needs was of minor importance in comparison to the needs of their husbands or their children. Middle and upper class women were provided for by their fathers and then, when married, their husbands, and were not encouraged to work outside the home. This was of course different from lower class women, who had to work to keep the family from starving. Women were not allowed to vote until the beginning of the twentieth century, and another emphasised notion of the nineteenth century that increased the suppression of women was the morally flawed view of sexuality. Women's sensuality was neglected unless viewed as filthy, while adultery committed by men was practically accepted. The first feminist movement was influenced by novels by female writers such as American Margaret Fuller, British Virginia Woolf and Norwegian Camilla Collett, who all contributed to the women's cause. They especially emphasised the individuality and sexuality of women, and this had great influence and made women become increasingly aware of their rights as individuals .

Title: Is reading a book better than watching its movie adaptation?
Paragraph: On the one hand, reading a book stimulates the brain, feeds your imagination and lets you become a part of what you're reading. When reading a novel, you get personally involved with the characters, you feel as if you connect to them on a personal level. Also, you get to visualize them, imagine what they may look like, you can picture their facial expressions and surroundings based on the author's description, and fill in the blanks using your own imagination. In a way, they are not only the writer's imaginings, but they become yours, too. A film adaptation is just one person's view of the book. Why should you have to limit yourself to that? And if you do happen to watch the movie adaptation first, one may argue that there is no room left for your own creative input when reading the book upon which the movie was based, because the film director's choice of actors will always be in the way. Also, you would already know what happens, so there is no thrill and anticipation to reach the end, is there?

Mainly in bigger cities the streets often become home for many young persons. But the law of the street is not an easy one. You have to fight for your life. If you are given no chance of earning your living in an honest way you steal the money you need or prostitute yourself to survive. And there he is the criminal of our days. When the law court judges these criminals and when they are put into prison things are getting worse: the law of the street is a hard one but the laws in prison are still harder and only the strongest survives. The problem in prison is that the prisoners are forced to live together on a very small space. This creates aggressions they cannot get rid of. The life in prison engraves on the convict's mind so that he is not able to live a normal life again after his release. He realizes that he will not get the help from society to reaccustom to a normal life: the vicious circle is closed .

Woe betide the child who cannot roll up his tongue by the age of three - he will have serious difficulties to obtain a place in nursery school, and he is most likely not to get out of it again at the age of six if he is not able to reach with his right hand over his head to touch his left ear. What for an unbiased observer might at first look like child's play later takes on a grim intellectual aspect. We have to prove our ability to add, to subtract, to spell, to enumerate the names of Bavarian kings and to twist our tongues in the effort to learn foreign languages if we wish to climb the ladder which leads from primary school over grammar school and university to the vertiginous heights of a successful career. But even at this point the horror might strike again - this time in the form of nightmares: who has not dreamt at least once in his life of taking his A-levels again and proving a downright failure?

Title: Europe 93: loss of sovereignty or birth of a nation?

Every people should be allowed to conduct its own affairs. That is sovereignity. But with the risk of being accused of pedantry I would like to put politics aside and discuss the concept on purely linguistic terms: Sovereignity is a conduct of one's own affairs . "One" can be a people, a person, a family, a congregation or a country club. The concept of sovereignity does not include an exclusive use of sovereignity by states or peoples as the ongoing discussion about a loss of sovereignity within the European Union tends to suggest .

Paragraph: There're some advantages of banning smoking in a restaurants. Firstly, It is believed that banning smoking can improve our health, as this will decrease the chance for breathing secondhand smoke. According to <R> , breathing secondhand smoke increases the risk of lung cancer and heart disease by about 25%. Tobacco-specific carcinogens have been found in the blood and urine of non-smokers exposed to environmental tobacco smoke. Therefore, banning smoking can not only help the smokers to reduce their opportunities of smoking, but also help the non-smokers to get away from breathing the second-hand smoking. And, it can improve the health for both types of person. Also, banning smoking in restaurants may be a gift to children and elderly, as their health is easily affected when they stay too long in a place with ' polluted air'.

Title: Why do people use (illegal) drugs ?
Paragraph: Today's young generation is slowly but continuously reducing on the hard drugs overuse. No one denies that drugs make irreparable harm to human's nervous system and that they deteriorate organism. The array of drugs has been recently changed by the young people. They tend to use milder stimulants such as herbal drugs or energizers. People want to take "healthy" pleasure from discos, or rather techno parties. That is why they have replaced crack cocaine and ecstasy with drinks such as "Red Bull" which induce the effect similar to drinking of three or four cups of coffee. The question of the legality of drugs may thus be soom invalid as the use of drugs will diminish .

Title: Cheating in colleges
Paragraph: Cheating is a special problem that requires careful treatment. Cheating wraps the student slowly and becomes habitual action. Title: Beware ! Alcohol is not innocuous
Paragraph: At present, we have become a population of notorious hypochondriacs who treat the slightest failure as a tragic disaster. Especially, people with lots of responsibility are easily susceptible to stress, thus tend to abuse alcohol. For them, an intoxicating drink is often the only infallible cure to relieve nervous tension. Alcohol was, in fact, man's original tranquilizer dating back to prehistoric times. Unfortunately, it alleviates stress temporarily without eliminating the real cause of the anguish. To make matters worse, the natural tendency of the human organism is towards stability and balance which alcohol invariably upsets. As a result, it affects in the long run virtually all aspects of human psychology. Additionally, it destroys both the inner organs and the central nervous system .

Title: In the 19th century, Victor Hugo said: "How sad it is to think that nature is calling out but humanity refuses to pay heed." Do you think it is still true nowadays?

Title: Marx once said that religion was the opium of the masses. If he was alive at the beginning of the 21st century, he would replace religion with television.
Paragraph: What is of primary importance to mention is, that people have become so obsessed with television that almost everyone, from the poorest to the richest one, has at least one television at home and usually more than one. It is really shocking to think how many hours a day one may watch television. In addition, some people even prefer watching television alone, rather than going out with friends, pursuing their hobbies or spending time with their families. As a result, people sentence themselves in alienation, isolation, sadness and depression. What is more, family bonds, love affairs and friendships are fragmented, which is a short-term consequence. As a long-term consequence, our whole society gradually undergoes sever fragmentation. To top it all, television damages both our psychological and physical health. For instance, one may gets depressed and mad and commit even a suicide or he may have to deal with a dangerous eye or brain damage due to the radiation television emits .

Title: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of banning smoking in restaurants.
Paragraph: Firstly, the advantage of banning smoking in restaurants is reduction of inhaling the second hand smoke by non-smokers. By reading the fact that there are 53,800 people who die every year from second hand smoke exposure we can see that by banning smoking in restaurants we could make our society much healthier than it is right now. A fact that proved that people are dying of the second hand smoke is a study which used magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) technology, and it was able to detect damage in the lungs of nonsmokers exposed to secondhand smoke. So by banning smoking in restaurants we could reduce risk of inhaling the second hand smoke by non-smokers .

Title: Some people say that in our modern world, dominated by science and technology and industrialisation, there is no longer a place for dreaming and imagination. What is your opinion?
Paragraph: Our ' industrial', ' technological ' life is full of stresses. People get tired of their work. For example, you are working at some factory standing all day long at the conveyer. You move automatically every day, you don't think. Very soon you begin to feel as if you were part of this conveyer. This monotonous work makes you sick, you don't want to be a machine. Your soul and your imagination need feelings and emotions. And you turn to something that was created by somebody else's imagination (of course, your imagination can also create but as I've already said not all of us are talented enough to express it). A lot of people love drama and they often go to different theatres to get an emotional supply there. Some of us prefer to go to different kinds of concerts and listen to music. Or, probably, one would buy a ticket to some comedy show or circus. Tastes differ. Our imagination prompts us where to go. It helps to find a way out of our stresses, it gives us hope for better future. No matter how ' technological ' or ' industrial ' life is there is always a place for dreaming and imagination. At least I believe in it .

Title: Morality and Leadership
Paragraph: In the New Testament we can read about a woman who was apprehended in the act of adultery. The defenders of the moral standards in those days - the Pharisees - brought the woman to Jesus, to see what he would say about her lewd conduct. According to the Mosaic law, the woman should be stoned. The Pharisees said this to Jesus and waited for his reply. It took a while for Jesus to answer, and when he opened his mout he said: "The one who is without sin may throw the first stone." The pious men turned silent, and one by one dropped their stones and disappeared quietly, the oldest ones first. These men, evidently, applied higher moral standards on the woman than on themselves. It was easier for them to condemn her than doing something about their own faults .

Title: Religion and rituals
Paragraph: What is most striking is the tragic impact religion has on innocent children. As a result of a suffocating religious upbringing kids are forced into a specific mode of thinking. By the age of ten most children are completely programmed . <*> . Religions, and rituals in particular can be terribly restrictive, as Pruyser <R> puts it: <*> . People are really made to believe that if you do not circumcise your baby boys the child will not have a bond with God (or Allah). The same goes for baptizing; if you do not baptize your children, they are off for a bad start. Being overwhelmed with certain beliefs and rites children are hardly able to think independently of their religion. They are forced to believe that you deserve to be punished if you do not keep the Sabbath or if you forget to pray five times a day (= Salat). Abraham Maslov, the famous humanist psychologist also mentioned the mind-narrowing dangers of rituals and religious strictness: <*> . It is evil to lead children to accept all these religious ' tricks', thereby limiting their possibilities to lead a life without being handicapped by rituals. One can develop mentally without these restrictive rituals .

Title: A defence of University cafeteria

One reason is that the cafeteria offers you so many opportunities to meet both friends and those who are to be made friends yet. Thus we can meet our old acquaintances on porpose, after having been seperated from them for two or more hours after the lunch-break, or we can endeavour to make new friends by a casual, sometimes not too accidential incounter. Which brings us to the main function of the cafeteria: it is a unique place of socialising where you are able to meet a great number of people .

Title: The Gulf War has shown us that it is still a great thing to fight for one's country
Paragraph: However it was difficult for us to have an objective vision of this war. The media were controlled in minute detail and the information was constantly screened. It may well be that Saddam Hussein was a fanatic who had to be stopped, but we would be naive if we thought that the Americans intervened in Kuwait only with a humanitarian intention. Take Peter Arnett's case, C.N.N. 's special correspondent in Bagdad. He wanted to show to the spectators the scale of the disaster after the Americans had bombed a dairy industry. That was the reason why he was considered as Hussein's sympathizer by Senator Alan Simpson, one of Bush's friends. Arnett insisted on the fact that he was innocent and reminded of the brave role played by his family during the Vietnam War. But is was too late! Perhaps he cared too much about the integrity of the media. That did not alter the fact that he lost his honour in the battle. The Senator even said that Arnett had given information to Hussein. That was why Bush decided that henceforth only the American soldiers could report the events of the war .

Title: The advantages and disadvantages of living in the city
Paragraph: Living in the city is not the perfect lifestyle for everybody. There are advantages and disadvantages of living in the city. This essay will consider some of the implications of city life, and a number of comparisons between life in the city and life in the country will be made. One comparison concerns the differences between the city and the country on the level of facilities. Several types of facilities will be discussed, such as hospitals, schools and entertainment. Another comparison concerns employment. For many people employment is a very important issue. This also becomes clear by looking at the facts concerning life in the city: one of the reasons why a lot of people live in the city is that there are more employment and career opportunities. A third comparison will be based on the incidence of crime and violence. It will appear that from this point of view, life in the country is to be prefered to life in the city. Fourth, the problem of keeping enough open spaces in the city will be discussed. By balancing the advantages with the disadvantages a conclusion will reached that strikes a happy balance between the two lifestyles .

Title: Europe 93: loss of sovereignty or birth of a nation?

It is argued by proponents of Europe that no sovereignity will be lost but sovereignity will merely be shifted to different levels of political organisation. That is to municipalities, regions and, of course Europe, whether it is called a continent or a nation. All this sounds very nice and permits me to introduce my personal favorite in the sea of words: subsidiarity. Subsidiarity makes nice sound even nicer: Subsidiarity means that whatever affairs can be reasonably conducted without the interference of the central power should be so conducted. Different affairs would thus be conducted by different people. This may first seems like a wonderful demonstration of flexibility and appropriateness. If this is the leading principle of the Europe of 1994 should not Europe be going into a good direction? Certainly, but the concept of subsidiarity is no less abused by the proponents of Europe than the concept of sovereignity is by the opponents .

However, our essence does not change, because it will always be stronger than everything. And we have to know to value it and try in somehow to develop it in a better way .

Title: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of students using credit cards
Paragraph: Credit card is very popular in Hong Kong. It is one kind of credit loan provided by the bank. When the cardholders pay the bills by using of credit card, they can obtain interest free before settlement of card statement such as within 30 days. The higher interest rate would be, however, charged if they overdue the payment. Because of competitive environment, the banks want to increase the customers and the market shares. They set us a simple procedure for application of credit card and provide benefit to the cardholders such as free gifts and special offers for some kinds of products. At the same time, students are one of their target. They foresee that the students, who study in University, would be a profession and earn high income in the future. Therefore, the sucessful for the application of the card are become easier. However, someone argue that the students, who the credit card, cannot earn benefit and may be harmful to them. Title: Most university degrees are theoretical and do not prepare students for the real world. They are therefore of very little value.
Paragraph: Take for example physics. We have learned that water boils at 100oC, the law of gravity, Newton's laws etc. We can't say that all these don't come in hand. How would we understand that the water boils, when, it makes bubbles and steams are coming out, if we hadn't read about it in physics? But, except of all these, wouldn't it be useful and valuable, if the school taught students how to find, for example, the most suitable job for them, or how to earn their living, or how to deal with their employer, or how to make a happy family and be a good parent? Isn't this, for the matter, the essence of education; to make each individual a whole and happy personality, able to confront life?
